It specifies technical requirements for vector allocation, message data modification, and the operational configuration of Extended Message Data for DWORD Memory Write transactions.

children.title: Section 7.7.1.8 Pending Bits Register for MSI (Offset 10h or 14h)
children.description: This section defines the Pending Bits Register for MSI, detailing the Read-Only (RO) bit-field structure used to indicate pending interrupt messages. It clarifies the architectural distinction between MSI’s internal configuration registers and MSI-X’s memory-mapped Pending Bit Array (PBA) structure.

children.title: Section 7.7.2 MSI-X Capability and Table Structure
children.description: This section defines the MSI-X Capability structure, detailing its architectural distinction from the MSI Capability by utilizing memory-mapped MSI-X Table and Pending Bit Array (PBA) structures. It specifies the configuration for Mask Bits and Pending Bits registers, addressing how Functions manage vector-level interrupt masking and reporting within the PCIe architecture.

children.title: Section 7.7.2.1 MSI-X Capability Header (Offset 00h)
children.description: This section defines the MSI-X Capability Header and the mathematical formulas for calculating memory-mapped addresses for MSI-X Table entries and Pending Bit Array (PBA) statuses. It specifies the QWORD and DWORD access rules for locating pending interrupts and details the structural requirements for the PCI Configuration Space Capability list.

children.title: Section 7.7.2.2 Message Control Register for MSI-X (Offset 02h)
children.description: This section details the **Message Control Register** for **MSI-X** within the **PCI Capability structure**, focusing on the **MSI-X Enable** bit, **Function Masking**, and the **Table Size** field. It provides technical specifications for hardware capability identification and clarifies configuration protocols for system software to manage interrupt delivery and vector masking.

children.title: Section 7.7.2.3 Table Offset/Table BIR Register for MSI-X (Offset 04h)
children.description: This section details the MSI-X Table Offset and Table BIR registers, defining the 32-bit BAR Indicator Register (BIR) and the table structure offset within a PCI resource. It specifies how to identify the memory space and address alignment required for locating the MSI-X vector table.

children.title: Section 7.7.2.4 PBA Offset/PBA BIR Register for MSI-X (Offset 08h)
children.description: This section specifies the PBA Offset and PBA BIR registers (Offset 08h) used for locating the MSI-X Pending Bit Array (PBA) within a device's memory-mapped configuration. It details how the PBA BIR identifies the associated Base Address Register (BAR) and how the PBA Offset provides a QWORD-aligned memory range to track pending interrupts.

children.title: Section 7.7.2.5 Message Address Register for MSI-X Table Entries
children.description: This section defines the structural requirements for the MSI-X Message Address Register and the Pending Bit Array (PBA) Offset/BIR register. It details bit-field specifications, alignment requirements for DWORD and QWORD memory transactions, and the mechanisms for mapping MSI-X table entries within a Function's configuration space.

children.title: Section 7.7.2.6 Message Upper Address Register for MSI-X Table Entries
children.description: This section specifies the format and functionality of the 32-bit Message Upper Address Register within MSI-X table entries, which defines the upper 32 bits of the destination address for 64-bit Memory Write interrupt messages. It answers questions regarding how PCIe devices address system memory for high-performance MSI-X interrupts and how firmware configures the target memory location for interrupt service routines.

children.title: Section 7.7.2.7 Message Data Register for MSI-X Table Entries
children.description: This section details the Message Data Register format within MSI-X Table entries, specifically defining the 32-bit field used for PCIe interrupt signaling. It addresses technical requirements for mapping data payloads to system-level interrupt controllers and explains how software configures these registers to ensure proper message delivery.

children.title: Section 7.7.2.8 Vector Control Register for MSI-X Table Entries
children.description: This section defines the **Message Upper Address** and **Message Data** fields within **MSI-X Table Entries**, specifying the requirements for **32-bit vs. 64-bit memory write transactions** and **DWORD payload** configurations. It addresses how these registers manage system-specified interrupt signaling and optional **Steering Tag** storage for Functions implementing the **TPH Requester Extended Capability**.

children.title: Section 7.7.2.9 Pending Bits Register for MSI-X PBA Entries
children.description: This section defines the **MSI-X Vector Control Register** structure, detailing bit-field management for the **Mask Bit**, **ST Lower**, and **ST Upper** fields. It provides technical specifications for interrupt masking, **TPH Requester** Steering Tag configurations, and mandatory software preservation requirements for **Reserved** bits.

children.title: Section 7.7.3 Secondary PCI Express Extended Capability
children.description: This section defines the Secondary PCI Express Extended Capability structure, detailing the Pending Bits Register for MSI-X Pending Bit Array (PBA) entries. It specifies implementation requirements for 8.0 GT/s+ links, Lower SKP OS Generation support, and lane-based error reporting, while outlining architectural constraints for Multi-Function Devices and RCRBs.

children.title: Section 7.7.3.1 Secondary PCI Express Extended Capability Header (Offset 00h)
children.description: This section details the Secondary PCI Express Extended Capability Header, defining fields such as the PCI Express Extended Capability ID, Capability Version, and Next Capability Offset. It provides technical specifications for link-level header identification and structure, answering how the extended capability chain is navigated and how secondary PCIe functions are registered within the configuration space.

children.title: Section 7.7.3.2 Link Control 3 Register (Offset 04h)
children.description: This section defines the **Link Control 3 Register** (offset 04h) within the **Secondary PCI Express Extended Capability** header, specifically detailing bit-level functionality for **Link Equalization**, **Interrupt Enable** controls, and **SKP OS Generation** vectors. It establishes the configuration requirements for triggering **8.0 GT/s+ equalizations** and managing standard PCIe link behavior in compatible Downstream Ports.

children.title: Section 7.7.3.3 Lane Error Status Register (Offset 08h)
children.description: This section specifies the **Lane Error Status Register** (Offset 08h) functionality, focusing on **Link Equalization Request** interrupt configuration for Downstream/Upstream ports and **Lower SKP OS Generation** vector settings for various **PCIe link speeds** (up to 128.0 GT/s). It defines bit-level attributes, enable conditions, and architectural requirements for managing timing/clock tolerance via SKP Ordered Set scheduling.

children.title: Section 7.7.3.4 Lane Equalization Control Register (Offset 0Ch)
children.description: This section specifies the Lane Equalization Control Register, detailing per-lane 8.0 GT/s equalization settings and the Lane Error Status Register for PCIe links. It defines register offsets, bit-field attributes, and physical lane mapping, answering how to configure transmitter presets and verify lane-based error status.

children.title: Section 7.7.4 Data Link Feature Extended Capability
children.description: This section defines the **Data Link Feature Extended Capability** bit field for **8.0 GT/s Receiver Preset Hint** values, detailing register attributes and **Link Equalization** logic for upstream and downstream ports. It specifies hardware initialization behavior, **Crosslink** support conditions, and the capture methodology for **EQ TS2** or preset request values during PCIe equalization sequences.

children.title: Section 7.7.4.1 Data Link Feature Extended Capability Header (Offset 00h)
children.description: This section details the PCIe Data Link Feature Extended Capability structure, covering the Extended Capability ID (0025h), Version, and Next Capability Offset fields. It defines the header format and register allocation necessary for identifying and traversing the Data Link Feature configuration space within the PCIe extended capability list.

children.title: Section 7.7.4.2 Data Link Feature Capabilities Register (Offset 04h)
children.description: This section defines the **Data Link Feature Capabilities Register** (offset 04h) and its role in mapping **Data Link Feature DLLP** fields to support features like **Scaled Flow Control** and **Immediate Readiness**. It specifies how Port capabilities are encoded for feature exchange, addressing how to determine a device’s supported data link functions during initialization.

children.title: Section 7.7.4.3 Data Link Feature Status Register (Offset 08h)
children.description: This section defines the **Data Link Feature Status Register** (Offset 08h), specifically addressing **Retimer L0p Exit Latency** requirements and **Local TLP per Half Flit** packing rules for **Flit Mode** operations. It details technical specifications for **Data Link Feature Exchange** enables and hardware initialization, providing essential configuration parameters for PCIe link negotiation and receiver compatibility.

children.title: Section 7.7.5 Physical Layer 16.0 GT/s Extended Capability
children.description: This section defines the register specifications and implementation requirements for the PCIe Physical Layer 16.0 GT/s Extended Capability, including TLP-per-half-flit encoding and Remote Data Link Feature support. It identifies specific rules for Function-based device eligibility and dictates port-specific behavior for establishing high-speed link parameters.

children.title: Section 7.7.5.1 Physical Layer 16.0 GT/s Extended Capability Header (Offset 00h)
children.description: This section details the Physical Layer 16.0 GT/s Extended Capability structure, including the register mapping for capability, control, status, and per-lane equalization settings. It defines the layout and offset architecture for 32-lane parity mismatch monitoring and link equalization control in PCIe Gen4 operations.

children.title: Section 7.7.5.2     16.0 GT/s Capabilities Register (Offset 04h)
children.description: This section details the 16.0 GT/s (PCIe Gen4) physical layer capabilities, focusing on Transmitter Preset/De-emphasis profiles and equalization signaling parameters. It answers how a device advertises its specific signal integrity requirements and negotiates optimal link-training settings for 16 GT/s operation.

children.title: Section 7.7.5.3     16.0 GT/s Control Register (Offset 08h)
children.description: This section defines the architectural structure of the Physical Layer 16.0 GT/s Extended Capability, specifically detailing the register offsets for the Capability Header, Capabilities Register, and Control Register. It specifies the unique PCI-SIG Extended Capability ID (0026h), versioning requirements, and linked-list pointer mechanics, answering questions regarding the identification and configuration of 16.0 GT/s PCIe interface capabilities.

children.title: Section 7.7.5.4     16.0 GT/s Status Register (Offset 0Ch)
children.description: This section defines the **16.0 GT/s Status Register**, specifying bit-level status indicators for **PCIe Transmitter Equalization** phases and completion. It addresses technical requirements for monitoring link equalization success and provides implementation mandates for **Multi-Function Upstream Ports**.

children.title: Section 7.7.5.5     16.0 GT/s Local Data Parity Mismatch Status Register (Offset 10h)
children.description: This section details the 16.0 GT/s Local Data Parity Mismatch Status Register, covering bit-field definitions, error logging, and parity detection mechanisms within PCI Express link layer traffic. It answers questions regarding how the physical layer identifies data integrity faults, where status bits are mapped at offset 10h, and how to interpret specific parity error triggers during high-speed data transmission.

children.title: Section 7.7.5.6     16.0 GT/s First Retimer Data Parity Mismatch Status Register (Offset 14h)
children.description: This section specifies the 16.0 GT/s First Retimer Data Parity Mismatch Status Register, defining how a 32-bit vector identifies individual physical lane parity errors during 16.0 GT/s or Flit Mode operations. It details register attributes, bit-level mapping for retimer diagnostics, and the appropriate software handling for error tracking across link speed changes.

children.title: Section 7.7.5.7     16.0 GT/s Second Retimer Data Parity Mismatch Status Register (Offset 18h)
children.description: This section defines the **16.0 GT/s Second Retimer Data Parity Mismatch Status Register** at offset **18h**, detailing error-reporting bits utilized for fault isolation within **PCIe 32 GT/s retimer links**. It answers how systems identify and log **data parity errors** traversing the secondary retimer interface during high-speed signal transmission.

children.title: Section 7.7.5.8 Physical Layer 16.0 GT/s Reserved (Offset 1Ch)
children.description: This section defines the reserved register space within the Physical Layer 16.0 GT/s Extended Capability structure at Offset 1Ch. It details specifications for PCIe hardware compliance, addressing how reserved bits are handled, documented, and architected to maintain backward compatibility during link-speed negotiations.

children.title: Section 7.7.5.9     16.0 GT/s Lane Equalization Control Register (Offsets 20h to 3Ch)
children.description: This section specifies the "16.0 GT/s Retimer Data Parity Mismatch Status" registers, detailing bit-level indicators for parity errors across physical link lanes. It provides technical definitions for diagnostic registers used to detect and track data corruption within PCIe Retimer paths during 16.0 GT/s and 8.0 GT/s Flit Mode operations.

children.title: Section 7.7.6 Physical Layer 32.0 GT/s Extended Capability
children.description: This section defines the PCIe Physical Layer 32.0 GT/s Extended Capability structure, detailing register attributes for Operating Port Direction and crosslink negotiation. It specifies implementation mandates for Downstream and Upstream Ports, conditions for supporting 32.0 GT/s link speeds, and requirements for Modified TS1/TS2 Ordered Set communication.

children.title: Section 7.7.6.1 Physical Layer 32.0 GT/s Extended Capability Header (Offset 00h)
children.description: This section defines the PCI Express 32.0 GT/s Physical Layer Extended Capability register structure, detailing the configuration of 32.0 GT/s capabilities, control, status, and per-lane equalization settings. It answers how to identify the capability header, locate modified TS data registers, and map equalization control registers for specific lanes up to Lane 31.

children.title: Section 7.7.6.2     32.0 GT/s Capabilities Register (Offset 04h)
children.description: This section details the Physical Layer 32.0 GT/s Extended Capability Register, defining header fields, capability versions, and next-capability offsets. It explains the bit-level implementation of 32 GT/s equalization features and modified Training Set (TS) usage modes, specifically addressing how to identify supported equalization bypasses and signaling protocols within the PCIe configuration space.

children.title: Section 7.7.6.3     32.0 GT/s Control Register (Offset 08h)
children.description: This section defines the **32.0 GT/s Control Register**, specifying configuration bits for **Equalization Bypass** and **No Equalization Needed** modes in PCIe Link Training. It addresses requirements for managing data rate transitions and selecting **Modified TS Usage Modes** during link initialization.

children.title: Section 7.7.6.4     32.0 GT/s Status Register (Offset 0Ch)
children.description: This section specifies the **32.0 GT/s Status Register** bits used to monitor PCI Express **Transmitter Equalization** progress, including Phase 1–3 completion status, **Link Equalization Request**, and **Transmitter Precoding** states. It dictates implementation requirements for Multi-Function Upstream Ports and defines how hardware signals the successful negotiation and execution of 32.0 GT/s equalization processes.

children.title: Section 7.7.6.5 Received Modified TS Data 1 Register (Offset 10h)
children.description: This section details the **Received Modified TS Data 1 Register**, defining hardware-level control bits for **32.0 GT/s Link Equalization** requests, **Modified TS** reception status, **Enhanced Link Behavior Control** fields, and **Transmitter Precoding** configurations. It specifies attribute behaviors (RO, RW1CS) and reset conditions (DL_Down) to govern PCIe Physical Layer state transitions and training sequences.

children.title: Section 7.7.6.6 Received Modified TS Data 2 Register (Offset 14h)
children.description: This section details the **Received Modified TS Data 2 Register (Offset 14h)**, defining the bit-field mapping for **Modified TS Ordered Sets**, including **Usage Mode**, **Information 1**, and **Vendor ID** fields. It specifies how these **RO (Read-Only)** bits store protocol-specific data received during training and establishes the default behavior when **PCI Express (Usage Mode 0)** is the sole supported protocol.

children.title: Section 7.7.6.7 Transmitted Modified TS Data 1 Register (Offset 18h)
children.description: This section defines the **Transmitted Modified TS Data 1 Register (Offset 18h)**, detailing the bit-field mapping for **Symbol 14–12 values** and the **Alternate Protocol Negotiation Status**. It specifies the initialization requirements, hardwiring conditions, and operational encodings necessary to determine if a Link successfully negotiated an alternate protocol during initialization.

children.title: Section 7.7.6.8 Transmitted Modified TS Data 2 Register (Offset 1Ch)
children.description: This section defines the Transmitted Modified TS Data 2 Register (Offset 1Ch), detailing the bit-level mapping of Modified TS2 Ordered Set symbols (8–11) to PCI Express configuration registers. It specifies the hardware default values, Read-Only (RO) attributes, and architectural requirements for capturing Training Set Message and Alternate Protocol Vendor IDs during the Configuration.Complete LTSSM state.

children.title: Section 7.7.6.9     32.0 GT/s Lane Equalization Control Register (Offset 20h)
children.description: This section defines the **32.0 GT/s Lane Equalization Control Register**, specifically detailing the **Alternate Protocol Negotiation Status** bits (25:24). It specifies the operational encodings, hardware requirements, and reset conditions for status reporting during **PCI Express link training** and protocol negotiation.

children.title: Section 7.7.7 Physical Layer 64.0 GT/s Extended Capability
children.description: This section defines the **Physical Layer 64.0 GT/s Extended Capability** register, specifically detailing **32.0 GT/s Lane Equalization Control**. It clarifies field attributes, hardware initialization, and directional usage for **Downstream and Upstream Ports** during **Link Equalization**, addressing how transmitter presets are configured, captured, and reported for system diagnostics.

children.title: Section 7.7.7.1 Physical Layer 64.0 GT/s Extended Capability Header (Offset 00h)
children.description: This section defines the Physical Layer 64.0 GT/s Extended Capability structure, detailing register allocation, header positioning for Upstream Port functions, and error reporting mechanisms for parity mismatches. It addresses how software manages 64.0 GT/s link configuration and error logging across the capability header, control, status, and per-lane equalization registers.

children.title: Section 7.7.7.2     64.0 GT/s Capabilities Register (Offset 04h)
children.description: This section defines the architectural parameters for 64.0 GT/s PCIe link operations, detailing bit-rate specific signaling, equalization requirements, and hardware support capabilities. It answers how a device advertises its compliance with PCI Express 6.0/7.0 physical layer standards and specifies the operational constraints required to achieve 64.0 GT/s data transfer rates.

children.title: Section 7.7.7.3     64.0 GT/s Control Register (Offset 08h)
children.description: This section defines the register-level interface for the 64.0 GT/s Physical Layer capability, detailing the structure and bit-level specifications of the Extended Capability Header, Capabilities Register, and Control Register. It serves as a technical reference to determine memory offsets, attribute behaviors (RO/RsvdP), and configuration requirements for managing link-speed capabilities within the PCIe 7.0 architecture.

children.title: Section 7.7.7.4     64.0 GT/s Status Register (Offset 0Ch)
children.description: This section defines the **64.0 GT/s Status Register**, detailing status bits for **Transmitter Equalization** completion, **Phase 1-3** success, and **Precoding** control. It provides technical specifications for managing PCIe link equalization sequences and signaling requirements for Multi-Function Upstream Ports.

children.title: Section 7.7.7.5     64.0 GT/s Lane Equalization Control Register (Offset 10h)
children.description: This section defines the 64.0 GT/s Lane Equalization Control Register, detailing bit-level fields for transmitter precoding status, precode requests, and "No Equalization Needed" status indicators. It outlines implementation requirements for Multi-Function Upstream Ports and specifies the register's structural layout regarding lane-count granularity and data size.

children.title: Section 7.7.8 Physical Layer 128.0 GT/s Extended Capability
children.description: This section specifies the "Physical Layer 128.0 GT/s Extended Capability" register, focusing on the Upstream Port 64.0 GT/s Transmitter Preset field and its HwInit/RO attributes. It defines technical encoding requirements, operational behaviors during Link Equalization across different crosslink configurations, and the mechanism for capturing Transmit Preset values within 128b/130b signaling.

children.title: Section 7.7.8.1 Physical Layer 128.0 GT/s Extended Capability Header (Offset 00h)
children.description: This section defines the PCIe Physical Layer 128.0 GT/s Extended Capability structure, detailing the register layout for equalization control, status, and header offsets. It addresses how parity errors are managed during speed transitions and specifies the register hierarchy for tracking Link Speed data integrity.

children.title: Section 7.7.8.2     128.0 GT/s Capabilities Register (Offset 04h)
children.description: This section defines the **128.0 GT/s Capabilities Register** within the **PCI Express Base Specification**, detailing bit-mapped vectors for **Supported Link Speeds**, **Lower SKP OS Generation**, and **Reception** capabilities. It provides the architectural mapping and **HwInit** attribute definitions required to identify physical layer data rate support for PCIe speeds of 128.0 GT/s or higher.

children.title: Section 7.7.8.3     128.0 GT/s Control Register (Offset 08h)
children.description: This section defines the control bitfields for 128.0 GT/s operations, detailing settings for equalization presets, receiver tuning, and transmitter signal parameters. It addresses configuration requirements for link training and signal integrity management necessary to achieve 128.0 GT/s signaling rates in PCIe architectures.

children.title: Section 7.7.8.4     128.0 GT/s Status Register (Offset 0Ch)
children.description: This section defines the 128.0 GT/s Status Register, detailing register bit assignments for PCIe transmitter equalization phases, completion signaling, and precoding states. It provides technical specifications for link training implementation, register attributes, and multi-function port requirements related to the 128 GT/s physical layer protocol.

children.title: Section 7.7.8.5     128.0 GT/s Lane Equalization Control Register (Offset 10h)
children.description: This section details the **128.0 GT/s Lane Equalization Control Register**, focusing on status bits for **Phase 2 and 3 equalization success** and the **Link Equalization Request** triggering mechanism. It defines hardware-specific implementation requirements for **Multi-Function Upstream Ports** and specifies bit-attribute behaviors, including **ROS (Read-Only Status)**, **RW1CS (Read/Write 1 to Clear Status)**, and **RsvdZ** constraints for PCIe link training orchestration.

children.title: Section 7.7.9 Flit Logging Extended Capability
children.description: This section specifies the architectural requirements for the Flit Logging Extended Capability structure, including implementation mandates for Ports, RCRBs, and Function 0 in Flit Mode. It defines the register layout, specifies physical lane mapping for FBER (Flit Bit Error Rate) measurements, and outlines the organizational interface for tracking and managing Flit-based transmission errors.

children.title: Section 7.7.9.1 Flit Logging Extended Capability Header (Offset 00h)
children.description: This section defines the structural configuration of the Flit Logging Extended Capability Header, detailing register layouts, Flit logging mechanisms, and PCIe-specific capability identifiers. It answers technical requirements regarding the offset-based register mapping, standard header field definitions, and the functional implementation of Flit-level transaction logging within the PCIe protocol.

children.title: Section 7.7.9.2 Flit Error Log 1 Register (Offset 04h)
children.description: This section defines the Flit Logging Extended Capability Header, detailing the register structure, PCI-SIG capability ID (0032h), versioning, and linked-list offset pointers. It provides technical specifications for identifying and navigating flit-related error reporting registers within the PCI Express configuration space.

children.title: Section 7.7.9.3 Flit Error Log 2 Register (Offset 08h)
children.description: This section details the **Flit Error Log 2 Register** at offset 08h, focusing on **error-reporting protocols**, **FLIT-level telemetry**, and **fault characterization** within the PCIe/CXL link layer. It addresses how the hardware captures specific **error status bits**, logs **corrupted flit payloads**, and provides the diagnostic data necessary for isolating **data integrity violations** or **link-layer synchronization failures**.

children.title: Section 7.7.9.4 Flit Error Counter Control Register (Offset 0Ch)
children.description: This section details the **Flit Error Counter Control Register (0Ch)** and **Flit Error Log 2 Register (08h)**, defining bit-level specifications for **FEC uncorrectable errors**, **ECC Group syndrome parity**, and **syndrome checks**. It addresses how link-level flit errors related to CRC mismatches and FEC group failures are logged and categorized within the Flit Mode interface.

children.title: Section 7.7.9.5 Flit Error Counter Status Register (Offset 0Eh)
children.description: This section defines the Flit Error Counter Status Register, detailing how to monitor link width during error events, manage interrupt generation triggers, and track the Flit Error Counter value. It explains hardware status monitoring mechanisms, including bit-field attributes, decrement rates based on encoding and link width specifications, and register reset triggers.

children.title: Section 7.7.9.6 FBER Measurement Control Register (Offset 10h)
children.description: This section details the configuration and operational parameters of the Forward Bit Error Rate (FBER) Measurement Control Register at offset 10h. It defines register bit-field definitions, enabling mechanisms for error rate monitoring, and sampling intervals required to validate signal integrity and link performance metrics.

children.title: Section 7.7.9.7 FBER Measurement Status 1 Register (Offset 14h)
children.description: This section defines the **FBER Measurement Control Register** (Offset 14h), detailing the bit-level configuration for enabling error measurements, clearing counters, and setting **FEC** (Forward Error Correction) granularity modes. It specifies operational requirements for **PAM4** signaling and outlines the control mechanism for **Retimers** within the **PCIe Link** architecture.

children.title: Section 7.7.9.8 FBER Measurement Status 2 Register (Offset 18h)
children.description: This section details the **FBER Measurement Status 2 Register (Offset 18h)**, defining the **Invalid Flit Counter** functionality and its **ROS (Read-Only Status)** attribute. It specifies how the counter increments during **FBER measurement enablement** and explains the reset mechanisms triggered by the **FBER Measurement Control Register**.

children.title: Section 7.7.9.9 FBER Measurement Status 3 Register (Offset 1Ch)
children.description: This section details the **FBER Measurement Status 3 Register (Offset 1Ch)**, covering bit-level definitions for **Bit Error Rate (BER)** monitoring and diagnostic status flags. It defines register accessibility, read-only bit functions, and hardware state indicators necessary for validating transmission integrity and error-rate performance.

children.title: Section 7.7.9.10 FBER Measurement Status 4 Register (Offset 20h)
children.description: This section defines the FBER Measurement Status 4 Register (Offset 20h), detailing 16-bit lane-specific counters for tracking FEC-correctable bit errors and SKP OS parity mismatches on PCIe lanes 2 and 3. It specifies non-rolling, read-only counter behavior and operational dependencies tied to the FBER Measurement Control Register.

children.title: Section 7.7.9.11 FBER Measurement Status 5 Register (Offset 24h)
children.description: This section details the **FBER (Forward Bit Error Rate) Measurement Status 5 Register** at **offset 24h**, specifying bit-field definitions, read-write access rules, and status flag behaviors. It answers questions regarding how to monitor real-time bit error rate performance data and interpret hardware diagnostic registers within the system memory map.

children.title: Section 7.7.9.12 FBER Measurement Status 6 Register (Offset 28h)
children.description: This section details the **FBER Measurement Status 6 Register** (Offset 28h), focusing on bit-level definitions for **Forward Bit Error Rate (FBER)** monitoring and real-time status reporting. It addresses how to interpret system error performance data and identifies the specific hardware flags used to track data integrity within the measurement subsystem.

children.title: Section 7.7.9.13 FBER Measurement Status 7 Register (Offset 2Ch)
children.description: This section details the **FBER (Forward Bit Error Rate) Measurement Status 7 Register (Offset 2Ch)**, defining the 16-bit **Correctable Counter** fields for PCI Express **Lane #8 and Lane #9**. It specifies the **Read-Only (RO)** register structure and attributes, providing the technical framework for monitoring per-lane link error statistics within the PCIe 7.0 specification.

children.title: Section 7.7.9.14 FBER Measurement Status 8 Register (Offset 30h)
children.description: This section details the **FBER (Forward Bit Error Rate) Measurement Status 8 Register** located at **offset 30h**, outlining bit-level definitions and status reporting mechanisms. It addresses how to interpret real-time error rate diagnostic data and diagnostic flag assertions within the performance monitoring architecture.

children.title: Section 7.7.9.15 FBER Measurement Status 9 Register (Offset 34h)
children.description: This section defines the **FBER Measurement Status 9 Register (Offset 34h)** within the PCI Express architecture, specifically detailing the bit-level mapping for **Lane #12 and Lane #13 Correctable Counters**. It specifies the **Read-Only (RO)** attributes and behavioral parity of these 16-bit performance monitoring counters relative to the Lane #0 implementation.

children.title: Section 7.7.9.16 FBER Measurement Status 10 Register (Offset 38h)
children.description: This section details the **FBER Measurement Status 10 Register (Offset 38h)**, defining the 32-bit layout for **Lane #14 and #15 Correctable Counters** within the **Device 3 Extended Capability** structure. It specifies register bit attributes and provides the architectural mapping for PCIe extended capability headers to support error-rate monitoring diagnostics.

children.title: Section 7.7.10 Device 3 Extended Capability Structure
children.description: This section details the PCIe Device 3 Extended Capability structure, covering its header, capabilities, control, and status registers. It defines the architectural parameters for device-specific configuration and management, answering questions regarding how to access, control, and monitor operating states for the associated PCIe function.

children.title: Section 7.7.10.1 Device 3 Extended Capability Header (Offset 00h)
children.description: This section specifies the layout and register definitions for the Device 3 Extended Capability structure, including the FBER Measurement Status 10 register for tracking per-lane correctable error counts. It serves as a technical reference for defining the architectural hierarchy, offset mapping, and bit-level implementation requirements for Lane #14 and #15 correctable error diagnostics within PCI Express Extended Capability headers.

children.title: Section 7.7.10.2 Device Capabilities 3 Register (Offset 04h)
children.description: This section defines the Device 3 Extended Capability header and Device Capabilities 3 register, specifying bit-level support for features such as 14-bit tags, L0p latency, and UIO Memory operations. It details the structure, register offsets, and functional capabilities necessary to identify and implement PCIe device-specific request routing and power management optimizations.

children.title: Section 7.7.10.3 Device Control 3 Register (Offset 08h)
children.description: This section defines the Device Control 3 Register (Offset 08h), detailing configuration bits for L0p exit latency, UIO memory transaction support, and OHC-E flit mode receiver capabilities. It answers technical specifications regarding completion roles, requester functionality, and maximum OHC-E Double Word (DW) support for hardware initialization.

children.title: Section 7.7.10.4 Device Status 3 Register (Offset 0Ch)
children.description: This section defines the Device Status 3 Register (Offset 0Ch) within the PCIe specification, detailing the UIO Request 256B Boundary Disable bit, field allocations, and link-related status indicators. It specifies address alignment requirements for memory requests and establishes the register’s hardware attributes and default configurations.

children.title: Section 7.7.11 Lane Margining at the Receiver Extended Capability
children.description: This section defines the "Lane Margining at the Receiver" Extended Capability, specifying mandate requirements for Downstream and Upstream Ports supporting PCIe Link speeds of 16.0 GT/s or higher. It outlines register attributes for Fabric Management (FM) versus Non-Fabric Management (NFM) states, the Remote L0p supported bit, and configuration rules for Switch ports and hierarchical Message Requests.

children.title: Section 7.7.11.1 Lane Margining at the Receiver Extended Capability Header (Offset 00h)
children.description: This section defines the 16-bit PCI Express Extended Capability ID, Version, and Next Capability Offset fields for the Lane Margining at the Receiver capability. It specifies the architectural header registers required for identifying and navigating the PCIe extended capability structure used to evaluate link signal integrity.

children.title: Section 7.7.11.2 Margining Port Capabilities Register (Offset 04h)
children.description: This section defines the "Lane Margining at the Receiver" Extended Capability header and the Margining Port Capabilities register within the PCIe configuration space. It specifies register layouts, bit-level attributes, and the "Margining uses Driver Software" flag, explaining how hardware capabilities are reported to software for link margining operations.

children.title: Section 7.7.11.3 Margining Port Status Register (Offset 06h)
children.description: This section details the Margining Port Status Register, specifically addressing its function in reporting hardware status, margin capability indicators, and lane-specific validation results. It defines bit-field definitions and operational conditions to answer how a system identifies and monitors physical layer signal integrity through PCIe margining procedures.

children.title: Section 7.7.11.4 Margining Lane Control Register (Offset 08h)
children.description: This section defines the **Margining Lane Control Register** and **Margining Port Status Register**, detailing the operational readiness states, bit-level attributes, and timing requirements for PCIe link margining. It addresses how software-driven initialization and link training speeds (16.0 GT/s+) dictate the status bits required to execute per-Lane margining commands.

children.title: Section 7.7.11.5 Margining Lane Status Register (Offset 0Ah)
children.description: This section defines the **Margining Lane Status Register** (Offset 0Ah), covering bit-field specifications for **Receiver Number**, **Margin Type**, **Usage Model**, and **Margin Payload**. It establishes the configuration parameters and reset behaviors required for per-lane signal integrity margining within the PCIe physical layer.

children.title: Section 7.7.12 ACS Extended Capability
children.description: This section defines the "Lane N: Margining Lane Status Register" within the ACS Extended Capability, detailing bit-field specifications for Receiver Number, Margin Type, Usage Model, and Payload Status. It answers how PCIe hardware reports lane margining status and reset requirements when a Port transitions to DL_Down status.

children.title: Section 7.7.12.1 ACS Extended Capability Header (Offset 00h)
children.description: This section defines the PCI Express ACS (Access Control Services) Extended Capability Header structure, specifying standardized layout requirements for the Capability ID (000Dh), versioning, and linked-list offset pointers. It provides the technical framework necessary to identify and traverse the ACS capability structure within PCIe configuration space.

children.title: Section 7.7.12.2 ACS Capability Register (Offset 04h)
children.description: This section defines the **Access Control Services (ACS) Capability Register**, detailing architectural support for features like **Source Validation**, **Translation Blocking**, and **P2P Redirect**. It specifies mandatory hardware implementations and read-only attributes required for **Root Ports**, **Switch Downstream Ports**, and **Multi-Function Devices** to ensure compliant PCIe peer-to-peer traffic management.

children.title: Section 7.7.12.3 ACS Control Register (Offset 06h)
children.description: This section defines the Access Control Services (ACS) Control Register, outlining mandatory enhancement capabilities, egress vector sizing, and specific configuration bits for request redirection, translation, and memory access control. It identifies whether a PCI Express component supports advanced flow control mechanisms such as source validation, P2P request redirection, and upstream forwarding.

children.title: Section 7.7.12.4 Egress Control Vector Register (Offset 08h)
children.description: This section defines the **Egress Control Vector Register** configuration, specifically addressing **ACS USP Memory Target Access Control** modes and **ACS Unclaimed Request Redirect Control** for PCIe Root Ports and Switch Downstream Ports. It specifies the functional requirements, bit-level encodings, and mandatory implementation constraints for managing upstream memory request routing and unsupported request (UR) handling.

children.title: Section 7.7.13 Captured Data Extended Capability
children.description: This section defines the Captured Data Extended Capability structure, specifying mandatory implementation requirements for PCIe Ports supporting 128.0 GT/s, Flit Mode, and specific device configurations. The text details register field allocation and clarifies technical use cases, specifically addressing how software gains visibility into link training parameters like N_FTS values when multiple retimers are present.

children.title: Section 7.7.13.1 Captured Data Extended Capability Header (Offset 00h)
children.description: This section defines the Captured Data Extended Capability Header, detailing the 32-bit register structure including the PCI Express Extended Capability ID (003Ah), Capability Version, and Next Capability Offset. It provides the technical specifications for navigating the PCIe configuration space linked list and identifying the format of the Captured Data Extended Capability structure.

children.title: Section 7.7.13.2 Captured Data Capabilities Register (Offset 04h)
children.description: This section specifies the bit-field definitions for the Captured Data Capabilities Register, detailing technical parameters such as supported data formats, recording trigger conditions, and buffer availability. It addresses how hardware-level data capture features are advertised and configured within the device's architectural register space.

children.title: Section 7.7.13.3 Captured Data Control Register (Offset 06h)
children.description: This section defines the Captured Data Control Register (Offset 06h), detailing how software selects specific internal physical layer or flow control data—such as N_FTS, link/lane numbers, or InitFC1 credits—for reporting. It specifies the bitwise encodings used to index these diagnostic metrics, answering which hardware parameters are accessible for system monitoring.

children.title: Section 7.7.13.4 Captured Data Register (Offset 08h)
children.description: This section defines the Captured Data Register (Offset 08h), detailing bit-level fields for N_FTS, link/lane parameters, and InitFC1 status. It answers how controller hardware captures and reports specific physical layer training and flow control initialization data for diagnostic and link-state monitoring purposes.

children.title: Section 7.7.13.4.1 Captured Data N_FTS, Link, Lane
children.description: This section details the **Captured Data Register** (Offset 08h) in the **PCI Express Base Specification**, specifically focusing on the **N_FTS, Link, and Lane** encoding enabled when the **Captured Data Select** field is set to 0000 0000b. It provides technical definitions for **EFM_CNTL1**, **EFM_CNTL2**, **Captured Link Number**, and **Captured Lane Number**, answering how the **LTSSM Configuration State** preserves specific training sequence data captured during Link Up.

children.title: Section 7.7.13.4.2 Captured Data InitFC1
children.description: This section defines the **Captured Data InitFC1** register structure, detailing bit-field mappings for **Enhanced Flit Mode (EFM)**, **Four Retimer Aware (FRA)** detection, and **Link/Lane numbering** captured during **LTSSM Configuration**. It specifies how **InitFC1 DLLP** values are recorded and stored for flow control initialization in PCIe architectures.

children.title: Section 7.8 Common PCI and PCIe Capabilities
children.description: This section details PCI and PCIe extended capability structures, including Power Budgeting, LTR, L1 PM Substates, AER, Resizable BAR, and PASID. It assists engineers in configuring advanced device features like error reporting, power management, resource allocation, and virtualization support within the PCIe configuration space.

children.title: Section 7.8.1 Power Budgeting Extended Capability
children.description: This section defines the Power Budgeting Extended Capability register structure, detailing bit fields for Enhanced Flit Mode (EFM) control, Flit Retimer Awareness (FRA), and captured link/lane configuration data. It provides technical specifications for identifying retimer counts and interpreting flow control initialization (InitFC1) values recorded during LTSSM states.

children.title: Section 7.8.1.1 Power Budgeting Extended Capability Header (Offset 00h)
children.description: This section defines the Power Budgeting Extended Capability header, detailing its optional implementation for PCI Express devices, integration requirements for Physical Functions (PFs), and aggregate power reporting for Virtual Functions (VFs). It explains how systems utilize these registers to ensure adequate cooling and power delivery, while providing the specific structure and offset layout for the Capability, Data Select, Control, and Data registers.

children.title: Section 7.8.1.2 Power Budgeting Data Select Register (Offset 04h)
children.description: This section defines the Power Budgeting Data Select Register, detailing bit-field definitions for selecting specific power management data values and power budget capability entries. It answers how software orchestrates the selection of power-related telemetry and configuration data within the Power Budgeting Extended Capability structure.

children.title: Section 7.8.1.3 Power Budgeting Control Register (Offset 06h)
children.description: This section defines the **Power Budgeting Control** and **Data Select** registers, specifically detailing the **Capability Version**, **Next Capability Offset**, and mechanisms for selecting **Power Budgeting Data** DWORDs. It addresses how system software enables extended power budgeting and manages auxiliary power allocations for PCI Express devices.

children.title: Section 7.8.1.4 Power Budgeting Data Register (Offset 08h)
children.description: This section defines the **Power Budgeting Data Register (Offset 08h)**, detailing its function as a read-only interface for retrieving device power usage metrics based on **Power Budgeting Data Select** register indexing. It specifies protocol requirements for handling **Extended Power Budgeting** entries, addressing register default states, null return conditions for out-of-bounds indices, and backward compatibility mandates for legacy software.

children.title: Section 7.8.1.5 Power Budgeting Capability Register (Offset 0Ch)
children.description: This section defines the mandatory reporting requirements for PCIe Power Budgeting Extended Capability, including D0/D3 power states, auxiliary power constraints, and thermal consumption limits. It specifies conditions for device power rail reporting, mutually exclusive PME/Auxiliary power configurations, and the regulatory logic applied when power values exceed form-factor or Aux_Current defaults.

children.title: Section 7.8.1.6 Power Budgeting Sense Detect Register (Offset 0Dh)
children.description: This section defines the **Power Budgeting Capability Register (Offset 0Dh)**, detailing bit-level status indicators for **system-allocated budgets**, **Extended Power Budgeting**, **Sense Detect**, and **Power Limit** features. It explains the functional dependencies between these bits and identifies specific **Power Disable** support models, answering how a device reports its power management capabilities and operational constraints.

children.title: Section 7.8.2 Latency Tolerance Reporting (LTR) Extended Capability
children.description: This section details the Latency Tolerance Reporting (LTR) Extended Capability, covering its implementation requirements for PCI Express Endpoints and Switch Upstream Ports. It provides technical specifications for managing platform latency information, multi-function device structure, and required link behavior control for LTR-capable hardware.

children.title: Section 7.8.2.1 LTR Extended Capability Header (Offset 00h)
children.description: This section defines the **LTR (Latency Tolerance Reporting) Extended Capability Header** structure, specifically detailing the **PCI Express Extended Capability ID (0018h)**, **Capability Version**, and **Next Capability Offset** fields. It provides the architectural mapping for identifying and navigating the LTR capability structure within the PCI Express configuration space for **Multi-Function RCiEPs**.

children.title: Section 7.8.2.2 Max Snoop Latency Register (Offset 04h)
children.description: This section defines the Max Snoop Latency Register, detailing bit-field specifications for setting maximum latency values for CPU snoop transactions. It answers how system software configures permissible latency thresholds to optimize cache coherency performance and power management within the PCIe architecture.

children.title: Section 7.8.2.3 Max No-Snoop Latency Register (Offset 06h)
children.description: This section defines the PCIe Max No-Snoop Latency Register, specifying parameters for device-requested latency constraints. It explains the function of the LatencyValue and LatencyScale fields and outlines software configuration requirements for Latency Tolerance Reporting (LTR) message updates.

children.title: Section 7.8.2.4 LTR Capabilities Register (Offset 08h)
children.description: This section defines the **LTR Capabilities Register** (offset 08h), detailing specifications for **Max No-Snoop Latency** scaling and **Function Level Reset (FLR)** behavior for **LTR Mechanism Enable**. It clarifies architectural requirements, mandatory hardware implementation triggers based on **Capability Version**, and the persistence of latency reporting settings following device resets.

children.title: Section 7.8.3 L1 PM Substates Extended Capability
children.description: This section details the **LTR Capabilities Register** within the **PCIe L1 PM Substates Extended Capability**, defining configuration for **Max No-Snoop Latency** scaling and the persistence of the **LTR Mechanism Enable** bit during **Function Level Reset (FLR)**. It specifies hardware requirements, register attributes, and the necessary timing for reflecting latency updates in **LTR Messages**.

children.title: Section 7.8.3.1 L1 PM Substates Extended Capability Header (Offset 00h)
children.description: This section defines the L1 PM Substates Extended Capability header, detailing the structure and mandatory fields (Capability ID 001Eh, versioning, and linked-list offsets) required for PCIe power management configuration. It addresses how the system identifies the L1 PM Substates capability and determines the structure's versioning and position within the PCI Express Extended Capability chain.

children.title: Section 7.8.3.2 L1 PM Substates Capabilities Register (Offset 04h)
children.description: This section defines the **L1 PM Substates Capabilities Register**, detailing bit-field specifications for **PCI-PM and ASPM L1.1/L1.2 substate support**, **Link Activation**, and **Port timing parameters** like **Common_Mode_Restore_Time**. It answers which power management substates a PCIe port supports and specifies the hardware-initialized timing requirements necessary for re-establishing common mode during L1.2 transitions.

children.title: Section 7.8.3.3 L1 PM Substates Control 1 Register (Offset 08h)
children.description: This section defines the L1 PM Substates Control 1 register, focusing on power management configuration parameters such as `T_POWER_ON` timing, `LTR_L1.2_THRESHOLD`, and enable bits for PCI-PM and ASPM L1.1/L1.2 substates. It specifies the register bit mapping, default values, and hardware implementation requirements for configuring link-level power-saving transitions in PCIe ports.

children.title: Section 7.8.3.4 L1 PM Substates Control 2 Register (Offset 0Ch)
children.description: This section defines the **L1 PM Substates Control 2 Register** (Offset 0Ch), covering specifications for the **T_POWER_ON Value** and **T_POWER_ON Scale** fields. It details the mandatory configuration requirements, synchronization constraints with **ASPM L1.2** and **PCI-PM L1.2 Enable** bits, and the calculation methodology for determining the post-exit interface activation delay.

children.title: Section 7.8.3.5 L1 PM Substates Status Register (Offset 10h)
children.description: This section defines the L1 PM Substates Status Register (Offset 10h), specifying its architectural requirements based on Capability Version and port type. It addresses when the Link Activation Status bit is implemented, its read-write-clear behavior, and its role within PCIe power management and status reporting.

children.title: Section 7.8.4 Advanced Error Reporting Extended Capability (AER)
children.description: This section defines the implementation requirements and structural constraints for the PCI Express Advanced Error Reporting (AER) Extended Capability, including specific rules for SR-IOV devices and Root Port/RCEC error handling. It clarifies when AER registers are required, how device types influence error message interpretation, and the conditions under which AER must be disabled for Virtual Functions.

children.title: Section 7.8.4.1 Advanced Error Reporting Extended Capability Header (Offset 00h)
children.description: This section defines the PCIe Advanced Error Reporting (AER) Extended Capability Header, detailing register layouts for the PCI Express Capability ID, Version, and Next Capability Offset. It identifies the formal specification requirements for error reporting management and defines how software navigates the linked list of PCIe extended capabilities.

children.title: Section 7.8.4.2 Uncorrectable Error Status Register (Offset 04h)
children.description: This section defines the **Advanced Error Reporting (AER) Extended Capability header** structure, detailing the **PCI Express Extended Capability ID (0001h)**, **Capability Version** requirements, and **Next Capability Offset** pointers. It provides the technical specifications necessary to identify and traverse the PCIe configuration space linked list for error reporting capabilities.

children.title: Section 7.8.4.3 Uncorrectable Error Mask Register (Offset 08h)
children.description: This section defines the **Uncorrectable Error Mask Register (offset 08h)**, detailing how individual PCI Express error reporting is suppressed via bit-masking to prevent error logging and Root Complex notification. It specifies implementation requirements for **VF (Virtual Function) registers**, including **RW1CS** versus **ROZ** attributes, and maps specific error conditions—such as **Completion Timeout**, **Poisoned TLP**, and **Receiver Overflow**—to their respective bit locations.

children.title: Section 7.8.4.4 Uncorrectable Error Severity Register (Offset 0Ch)
children.description: This section defines the Uncorrectable Error Severity Register bit assignments, covering masking controls for specialized conditions such as Poisoned TLP Egress, DMWr Request Egress, IDE check failures, and TLP translation errors. It specifies the configuration, implementation requirements, and access attributes (RWS/VF ROZ) for these error-handling bits within the PCIe Advanced Error Reporting (AER) framework.

children.title: Section 7.8.4.5 Correctable Error Status Register (Offset 10h)
children.description: This section specifies the layout, bit-level definitions, and clearing mechanisms for the PCI Express Correctable Error Status Register. It defines the implementation requirements for error reporting, including TLP translation, link-layer protocol errors, and SR-IOV specific logging rules for Physical and Virtual Functions.

children.title: Section 7.8.4.6 Correctable Error Mask Register (Offset 14h)
children.description: This section defines the **Correctable Error Mask Register (Offset 14h)**, detailing bit-level control for suppressing specific PCI Express error reporting to the Root Complex. It provides technical specifications for masking parameters including **Receiver Errors, Bad TLPs/DLLPs, Replay Timer Timeouts, and Header Log Overflow**, clarifying register behavior for both Physical Functions (PF) and Virtual Functions (VF).

children.title: Section 7.8.4.7 Advanced Error Capabilities and Control Register (Offset 18h)
children.description: This section defines bit-level control and reporting mechanisms within the Advanced Error Capabilities and Control register, specifically detailing mask configuration, ECRC handling, and header log management. It addresses how PCIe devices manage error reporting, overflow conditions, and status pointer initialization for both Physical Functions (PFs) and Virtual Functions (VFs).

children.title: Section 7.8.4.8 Header Log Register (Offset 1Ch)
children.description: This section details the **Header Log Register (Offset 1Ch)** architecture, documenting bit-level specifications for **Flit Mode** status, **Logged TLP Size**, and **RC ECS Handling** capabilities. It defines how the 16-byte register captures and formats **TLP headers** to enable accurate error reporting and diagnostic analysis within the PCIe specification.

children.title: Section 7.8.4.9 Root Error Command Register (Offset 2Ch)
children.description: This section defines the **Root Error Command Register** (offset 2Ch) and the **Header Log Register**, detailing how Root Ports and Root Complex Event Collectors manage error reporting. It specifies the configuration of interrupt generation for Correctable, Non-Fatal, and Fatal PCI Express error messages and outlines the functional dependencies required for system error notification and TLP header capture.

children.title: Section 7.8.4.10 Root Error Status Register (Offset 30h)
children.description: This section defines the **Root Error Status Register (Offset 30h)**, detailing how Root Ports log and report **ERR_COR, ERR_NONFATAL, and ERR_FATAL** messages and internal errors. It explains the mechanics of **first error bit** signaling, **Requester ID** logging in the Error Source Identification Register, and the software-based bit-clearing procedure required for managing **PCI Express Advanced Error Reporting (AER)**.

children.title: Section 7.8.4.11 Error Source Identification Register (Offset 34h)
children.description: This section defines the Error Source Identification Register (Offset 34h), detailing the management of fatal and correctable error messages through bit-specific status flags like ERR_COR Subclass and SFW Handling. It specifies register attributes and behaviors for hardware signaling, firmware handling, and interrupt routing, clarifying how system-level error reporting is tracked and identified.

children.title: Section 7.8.4.12 TLP Prefix Log Register (Offset 38h)
children.description: This section defines the TLP Prefix Log Register and Error Source Identification Register, detailing how PCIe devices capture Requester IDs for ERR_COR, ERR_NONFATAL, and ERR_FATAL messages. It explains the bit-level organization of captured End-End TLP Prefixes and specifies the conditions under which these registers provide valid diagnostic data for error reporting.

children.title: Section 7.8.5 Enhanced Allocation Capability Structure (EA)
children.description: This section details the Enhanced Allocation (EA) Capability structure within PCIe configuration space, covering the First/Second DWord headers and per-entry resource allocation formats. It addresses how Type 1 functions define base address register (BAR) requirements and manage memory or I/O resource assignments through extended capability entries.

children.title: Section 7.8.5.1 Enhanced Allocation Capability First DW (Offset 00h)
children.description: This section details the **Enhanced Allocation capability** structure, specifically defining the **TLP Prefix Log Registers** and the **First DW** layout containing the **Capability ID (14h)**, **Next Capability Pointer**, and **Num Entries** fields. It provides technical specifications for register bit-fields, attributes, and hardware initialization requirements for PCI Express functions implementing this allocation mechanism.

children.title: Section 7.8.5.2 Enhanced Allocation Capability Second DW (Offset 04h) [Type 1 Functions Only]
children.description: This section defines the **Enhanced Allocation Capability Second DW** register for **PCI Express Type 1 Functions**, detailing specific **Capability ID** and **Next Capability Pointer** structures. It clarifies how hardware manages resource allocation mappings and multi-function device addressing within the PCI configuration space.

children.title: Section 7.8.5.3 Enhanced Allocation Per-Entry Format (Offset 04h or 08h)
children.description: This section defines the "Enhanced Allocation Per-Entry Format" and the mandatory "Second DW" structure for Type 1 Functions within the PCI Express Enhanced Allocation Capability. It specifies register bit layouts for "Num Entries," "Fixed Secondary Bus Number," and "Fixed Subordinate Bus Number," addressing how to configure bus management when Functions utilizing Enhanced Allocation are positioned downstream.

children.title: Section 7.8.6 Resizable BAR Extended Capability
children.description: Section 7.8.6 specifies the Resizable BAR Extended Capability for PCIe, detailing how hardware advertises resource size support and how system software dynamically programs optimal sizes via Control registers. This section addresses hardware-software negotiation protocols, BAR memory mapping constraints, and register bit-field definitions for 32-bit and 64-bit base address configurations.

children.title: Section 7.8.6.1 Resizable BAR Extended Capability Header (Offset 00h)
children.description: This section defines the **PCIe Resizable BAR Extended Capability Header**, detailing the **Capability ID**, **Capability Version**, and **Next Capability Offset** fields. It answers how system software identifies the presence and version of Resizable BAR support to manage flexible memory-mapped I/O base address register (BAR) resizing within the PCI configuration space.

children.title: Section 7.8.6.2 Resizable BAR Capability Register
children.description: This section defines the **Resizable BAR Extended Capability Header** and **Resizable BAR Capability Register** structure, detailing the **PCI Express Extended Capability ID (0015h)**, **versioning**, and **next-capability offsets**. It specifies the bitmask mapping for supported power-of-two **BAR sizes** (ranging from 1 MB to 64 MB and beyond) to determine system-level memory addressing capabilities.

children.title: Section 7.8.6.3 Resizable BAR Control Register
children.description: Section 7.8.6.3 defines the Resizable BAR Control Register, detailing bit-level support for memory capacities ranging from 8 TB to 8 EB. This technical specification explains how to identify hardware-supported BAR sizes and configure specific Base Address Register indices for PCIe device memory mapping.

children.title: Section 7.8.7 VF Resizable BAR Extended Capability
children.description: This section defines the **VF Resizable BAR Extended Capability** register, specifically detailing the bit-field definitions for supporting **Phase-Locked Loop (PLL) or memory-mapped BAR sizes** ranging from 16 PB to 8 EB. It identifies how to query a Function's hardware-supported address space capacities through its **Read-Only (RO)** capability bits.

children.title: Section 7.8.7.1 VF Resizable BAR Extended Capability Header (Offset 00h)
children.description: This section defines the PCIe VF Resizable BAR Extended Capability Header, detailing register layouts, revision numbers, and capability IDs. It addresses how to identify and configure Virtual Function (VF) resource sizing requirements and base address register (BAR) structure within SR-IOV environments.

children.title: Section 7.8.7.2 VF Resizable BAR Capability Register (Offset 04h)
children.description: This section defines the **VF Resizable BAR Capability Register**, detailing its bit-field layout for managing **SR-IOV** Virtual Function memory resource allocation. It specifies how to identify supported **BAR sizes**, configure **BAR indices**, and determine memory-mapped I/O requirements for virtualized PCI Express devices.

children.title: Section 7.8.7.3 VF Resizable BAR Control Register (Offset 08h)
children.description: This section defines the **VF Resizable BAR Control Register** (Offset 08h) and the **VF Resizable BAR Extended Capability Header**, detailing the configuration of **SR-IOV Virtual Function (VF) base address registers**. It specifies header fields, functional offsets, and memory size control requirements, clarifying how VF BAR resource allocation differs from standard Physical Function (PF) resizable BAR implementations.

children.title: Section 7.8.8 ARI Extended Capability
children.description: This section defines the PCIe Alternative Routing-ID Interpretation (ARI) Extended Capability structure, covering the formatting of the Header, Capability Register, and Control Register. It addresses how to configure and enable ARI to allow for increased Function counts per device, specifically through the expansion of the PCI Function number space.

children.title: Section 7.8.8.1 ARI Extended Capability Header (Offset 00h)
children.description: This section details the ARI Extended Capability Header, defining the structure of the PCI Express Extended Capability ID (000Eh), versioning, and next-capability pointers. It specifies register attributes and technical requirements for mandatory ARI implementation in SR-IOV devices and ARI-capable functions.

children.title: Section 7.8.8.2 ARI Capability Register (Offset 04h)
children.description: This section details the **ARI Capability Register**, specifically the **MFVC** and **ACS Function Groups** capabilities and the **Next Function Number** linked-list mechanism. It defines how SR-IOV devices manage function-level arbitration and enumeration, clarifying requirements for Bus Number consumption and multi-function device hierarchy.

children.title: Section 7.8.8.3 ARI Control Register (Offset 06h)
children.description: This section defines the ARI Control Register fields, specifically addressing bit-level configurations for MFVC and ACS Function Group enables and Function Group assignment. It details the mechanisms for functions to interpret arbitration tables and egress control vectors, specifying hardware-required defaults and conditional enable logic for PCI Express ARI devices.

children.title: Section 7.8.9 PASID Extended Capability Structure
children.description: This section defines the **PASID Extended Capability Structure** for PCIe devices, covering the **ARI Control Register** and its bit fields for **MFVC** and **ACS Function Group** configuration. It specifies how to enable Function Group interpretation and identifies whether an Endpoint supports **PASID TLP Prefixes** or **OHC with PASID** in fabric-managed environments.

children.title: Section 7.8.9.1 PASID Extended Capability Header (Offset 00h)
children.description: This section defines the PASID Extended Capability header structure, specifying its independent application to Endpoint Functions and Physical Functions (PFs) with their associated Virtual Functions (VFs). It clarifies architectural requirements, including Requester ID handling for multi-function devices and the optional nature of ATS and PRI support.

children.title: Section 7.8.9.2 PASID Capability Register (Offset 04h)
children.description: This section defines the PASID Capability Register bit layout, including the PASID Capability ID, Next Capability Pointer, and PASID Control/Status fields. It specifies the architectural requirements for enabling Process Address Space ID (PASID) support and managing interface-level configuration within the PCIe capability structure.

children.title: Section 7.8.9.3 PASID Control Register (Offset 06h)
children.description: Section 7.8.9.3 defines the PASID Capability Register, detailing configuration bits for Execute Permission, Privileged Mode, Translated Requests, and the Max PASID Width field. This section specifies endpoint hardware capabilities and constraints for PASID-based memory transactions within the PCI Express architecture.

children.title: Section 7.8.10 FRS Queueing Extended Capability
children.description: This section defines the FRS (Fast Remote Shutdown) Queueing Extended Capability, detailing register-level specifications for capabilities, status tracking, control operations, and message queuing. It provides technical documentation necessary for implementing and managing hardware-based FRS message delivery systems within a PCIe/system fabric environment.

children.title: Section 7.8.10.1 FRS Queueing Extended Capability Header (Offset 00h)
children.description: This section defines the FRS Queueing Extended Capability header structure (ID 0021h) required for PCIe Root Ports and Root Complex Event Collectors using FRS Queueing. It specifies register offsets, bit-field requirements for capability versions and pointers, and mandates the implementation of MSI/MSI-X interrupts.

children.title: Section 7.8.10.2 FRS Queueing Capability Register (Offset 04h)
children.description: This section defines the **FRS Queueing Capability Register**, detailing architectural support for **Fast Role Swap (FRS)** signaling and **message queueing** constraints within the **USB Power Delivery** physical layer. It specifies bit-level field definitions to address questions regarding device eligibility for FRS, maximum queued message capacity, and hardware-level state machine handshake protocols.

children.title: Section 7.8.10.3 FRS Queueing Status Register (Offset 08h)
children.description: This section defines the FRS Queueing Capability Register, specifically detailing the FRS Queue Max Depth and FRS Interrupt Message Number parameters. It establishes hardware-initialized constraints and configuration requirements for managing queue capacity and mapping interrupt vectors for MSI/MSI-X mechanisms.

children.title: Section 7.8.10.4 FRS Queueing Control Register (Offset 0Ah)
children.description: This section specifies the bit-field definitions, configuration protocols, and operational modes for the FRS Queueing Control Register located at offset 0Ah. It addresses functional requirements regarding traffic prioritization, buffer management, and packet arbitration parameters within the FRS hardware interface.

children.title: Section 7.8.10.5 FRS Message Queue Register (Offset 0Ch)
children.description: This section specifies the PCIe FRS (FHRP/Functional Reporting Service) Message Queue Register interface, detailing status reporting, interrupt configuration, and queue management via the FRS Queueing Status, Control, and Data registers. It defines the mechanisms for tracking message reception and overflow, managing FRS interrupt triggers, and extracting queued metadata like Function ID, Reason, and Queue Depth.

children.title: Section 7.8.11 Flattening Portal Bridge (FPB) Capability
children.description: This section defines the Flattening Portal Bridge (FPB) Capability structure and the FRS Message Queue Register, detailing how PCIe bridges manage FRS message tracking. It specifies field attributes and memory-mapped offsets necessary to identify how hardware handles requester IDs, message depth, and vector control registers for internal routing.

children.title: Section 7.8.11.1 FPB Capability Header (Offset 00h)
children.description: This section defines the Fixed Platform BAR (FPB) Capability Header, detailing mandatory register fields including Capability ID, Next Capability Offset, and Version. It specifies the structural metadata required to identify, traverse, and configure FPB capabilities within PCIe configuration space.

children.title: Section 7.8.11.2 FPB Capabilities Register (Offset 04h)
children.description: Section 7.8.11.2 defines the Fabric-Port-Bundle (FPB) Capabilities Register, detailing the hardware-initialized bitfields that specify supported decoding mechanisms for Revision Identifier (RID), MEM Low, and MEM High vectors. This section provides the technical specifications for identifying registry field offsets and vector size configurations required to implement FPB address decoding within the PCIe architecture.

children.title: Section 7.8.11.3 FPB RID Vector Control 1 Register (Offset 08h)
children.description: This section defines the FPB RID Vector Control 1 Register, detailing bit-level specifications for FPB RID Decode Mechanism enabling and configuration of vector granularity. It answers how to programmatically control memory decoding mechanisms and set alignment requirements for RID (Requester ID) vectors within a PCI Express Fabric Port.

children.title: Section 7.8.11.4 FPB RID Vector Control 2 Register (Offset 0Ch)
children.description: This section defines the **FPB RID Vector Control 2 Register**, detailing **RID Vector Start offsets** and **Granularity** encodings (64 or 256 RIDs) for **PCIe Fabric-to-PCIe Bridge (FPB)** systems. It specifies hardware implementation requirements, **ARI Forwarding** dependencies, and programming constraints for aligning **RID-based decoding** ranges.

children.title: Section 7.8.11.5 FPB MEM Low Vector Control Register (Offset 10h)
children.description: This section defines the **FPB MEM Low Vector Control Register** and **FPB RID Vector Control 2 Register**, detailing the configuration of **Type 1 to Type 0** RID conversion offsets and **MEM Low** decode mechanism enablement. It provides technical specifications for **RID Secondary Start** fields, **Vector Granularity** requirements, and hardware implementation constraints for PCIe Fabric-Port Bridges (FPB).

children.title: Section 7.8.11.6 FPB MEM High Vector Control 1 Register (Offset 14h)
children.description: This section defines the **FPB MEM High Vector Control 1 Register**, detailing the **granularity** and **start address** configuration for memory vector decoding. It specifies hardware alignment requirements, field attributes (RW/RO), and default values necessary for implementing memory vector decoding mechanisms.

children.title: Section 7.8.11.7 FPB MEM High Vector Control 2 Register (Offset 18h)
children.description: This section defines the **FPB MEM High Vector Control 2 Register (Offset 18h)**, specifically detailing the **FPB MEM High Vector Start Upper** field used for configuring 64-bit base address alignment. It specifies hardware constraints, **natural alignment requirements** based on the **FPB MEM High Vector Granularity**, and the conditions governing register accessibility and default values.

children.title: Section 7.8.11.8 FPB Vector Access Control Register (Offset 1Ch)
children.description: This section defines the FPB Vector Access Control Register (Offset 1Ch), detailing the bit-field architecture and configuration requirements for managing RID, MEM Low, and MEM High vector offsets. It clarifies register attributes, granularity constraints, and the access mechanisms necessary for performing data operations via the FPB Vector Access Data register.

children.title: Section 7.8.11.9 FPB Vector Access Data Register (Offset 20h)
children.description: This section specifies the **FPB Vector Access Data Register**, defining its architectural layout, including the **FPB Vector Select** field and **Vector Access Offset** range. It details how to perform doubleword (DW) reads and writes to specific FPB vectors using the **Vector Access Offset Register** to index memory locations.

children.title: Section 7.8.12 Flit Performance Measurement Extended Capability
children.description: This section defines the PCIe Flit Performance Measurement Extended Capability, detailing register structures for the header, capability, control, and status interfaces. It addresses how hardware tracks Flit-based latency and LTSSM state durations to facilitate performance analysis and telemetry in high-speed interconnects.

children.title: Section 7.8.12.1 Flit Performance Measurement Extended Capability Header (Offset 00h)
children.description: This section defines the structural layout and architectural requirements for the Flit Performance Measurement Extended Capability, including register definitions and optional LTSSM performance monitoring components. It addresses queries regarding capability support, register accessibility, implementation rules for Flit Mode, and the impacts of hardware-specific register counts on the overall capability structure.

children.title: Section 7.8.12.2 Flit Performance Measurement Capability Register (Offset 04h)
children.description: This section defines the Flit Performance Measurement Extended Capability, detailing the register structure for the Capability Header and specific operational parameters including the Flit Performance Interrupt Vector and LTSSM Tracking Register Count. It specifies technical memory offsets, PCI-SIG defined capability IDs, and field attributes to facilitate hardware configuration and performance monitoring within the PCIe subsystem.

children.title: Section 7.8.12.3 Flit Performance Measurement Control Register (Offset 08h)
children.description: This section details the **Flit Performance Measurement Control Register**, defining bit-field configurations for latency monitoring, LTSSM state transition tracking, and interrupt thresholds. It addresses requirements for managing measurement enables, instance tracking limitations, and synchronization constraints during hardware-based flit performance diagnostics.

children.title: Section 7.8.12.4 Flit Performance Measurement Status Register (Offset 0Ch)
children.description: This section details the **Flit Performance Measurement Status Register**, covering **Flit Latency Tracking** status, instance counts, and interrupt generation bits. It explains how to monitor tracking progress, identify errors like **counter overflow**, and interpret the **RW1C (Read/Write 1 to Clear)** interrupt status bit.

children.title: Section 7.8.12.5 LTSSM Performance Measurement Status Register (Offsets 10h to 20h)
children.description: This section defines the **LTSSM Performance Measurement Status Register**, detailing bits for tracking state transitions, latency calculations, and interrupt triggers. It answers how to monitor PCIe link performance via hardware-reported status codes, cumulative/peak latency measurement logic, and state transition interrupt configuration.

children.title: Section 7.8.13 Flit Error Injection Extended Capability
children.description: This section details the Flit Error Injection Extended Capability, covering LTSSM state transition tracking, interrupt generation, and latency measurement registers. It addresses how to manage, monitor, and configure error injection parameters specifically for PCIe Flit Mode operations while providing implementation warnings regarding FEC limitations.

children.title: Section 7.8.13.1 Flit Error Injection Extended Capability Header (Offset 00h)
children.description: This section specifies the register structure and field definitions for the Flit Error Injection Extended Capability (ID 0034h) header within the PCIe configuration space. It identifies the capability versioning and offset mapping necessary for configuring and monitoring flit and ordered set error injection protocols.

children.title: Section 7.8.13.2 Flit Error Injection Capability Register (Offset 04h)
