children.description: This section defines the **Flit Error Injection Capability Register** within the PCIe specification, detailing bit-level architecture, **FLIT-based error messaging**, and **RAS (Reliability, Availability, and Serviceability)** features. It specifies the hardware trigger mechanisms, status reporting protocols, and implementation requirements for validating **link-layer fault isolation** within the 256-byte/512-byte flit mode.

children.title: Section 7.8.13.3 Flit Error Injection Control 1 Register (Offset 08h)
children.description: This section details the **Flit Error Injection Control 1 Register** (offset 08h), covering configuration fields for **PCI Express Extended Capabilities**, including **error injection enablement**, **data rate**, **error count**, and **type-specific injection parameters**. It defines hardware behavior for **transmitted and received flit corruption** and specifies register bit-level attributes for debugging link-layer data integrity.

children.title: Section 7.8.13.4 Flit Error Injection Control 2 Register (Offset 0Ch)
children.description: This section defines the **Flit Error Injection Control 2 Register**, detailing the configuration of **consecutive error injection**, **error types**, and **magnitude parameters** for link-level fault simulation. It specifies register bit-fields, functional attributes, and operational constraints for injecting errors into transmitted or received Flits.

children.title: Section 7.8.13.5 Flit Error Injection Status Register (Offset 10h)
children.description: This section details the **Flit Error Injection Status Register**, covering **error injection status tracking** for both **Tx and Rx ports**, including progress indicators and abort conditions. It defines technical fields for **Error Magnitude** and **byte-distance parameters**, answering how the hardware reports the completion state and configuration validity of simulated **uncorrectable flit errors**.

children.title: Section 7.8.13.6 Ordered Set Error Injection Control 1 Register (Offset 14h)
children.description: This section defines the **Ordered Set Error Injection Control 1 Register**, detailing bit-mapped parameters for configuring hardware to inject link-level errors into transmission and reception paths. It specifies operational constraints, state-machine triggers (e.g., L0, Recovery, Equalization phases), and specific ordered set targets (e.g., SKP, TS, EIEOS) required for protocol compliance testing and link debugging.

children.title: Section 7.8.13.7 Ordered Set Error Injection Control 2 Register (Offset 18h)
children.description: This section defines the **Ordered Set Error Injection Control 2 Register**, specifying the bit-level configuration for targeting specific Ordered Sets (TS0-TS2, SKP, EIEOS, EIOS, SDS) and LTSSM states (Polling, Configuration). It details parameters for error injection frequency, spacing, and duration, addressing how hardware validates reliability by simulating protocol-level corruption.

children.title: Section 7.8.13.8 Ordered Set Error Tx Injection Status Register (Offset 1Ch)
children.description: This section defines the **Ordered Set Error Tx Injection Status Register** and **Ordered Set Error Injection Control 2 Register**, detailing bit-field mappings for error injection across specific lanes, bytes, and PCIe link states. It provides technical specifications for monitoring the injection status of ordered sets—including TS1, TS2, SKP, and EIOS—and configuring targeted error injection parameters for physical layer testing.

children.title: Section 7.8.13.9 Ordered Set Error Rx Injection Status Register (Offset 20h)
children.description: This section defines the **Ordered Set Error Rx Injection Status Register (Offset 20h)**, which utilizes **ROS (Read-Only Status)** fields to track the transmission injection status of various PCIe ordered sets and link states. It provides technical specifications for monitoring error injection across states ranging from **TS0/TS1/TS2** and **SKP** to specific **Recovery.Equalization** phases.

children.title: Section 7.8.14 NOP Flit Extended Capability
children.description: This section defines the NOP Flit Extended Capability architecture, detailing the register structures and status bit encodings for monitoring Ordered Set error injection across various link states (e.g., TS1, TS2, Recovery, L0). It specifies implementation requirements for Downstream Ports, Upstream Function 0, and RCRBs, addressing how error injection status is signaled, managed, and cleared during Flit-mode operations.

children.title: Section 7.8.14.1 NOP Flit Extended Capability Header
children.description: This section defines the NOP Flit Extended Capability Header (ID 0037h), detailing its register structure, bit-field definitions (Capability Version, Next Capability Offset), and hardware initialization requirements for PCI Express configuration space. It serves as the primary technical specification for identifying and navigating the NOP Flit capability structure within a system's linked list of capabilities.

children.title: Section 7.8.14.3 NOP Flit Control 1 Register
children.description: This section defines the NOP Flit Capabilities and Control 1 registers, detailing bit-field mappings and configurations for NOP.Debug and NOP.Vendor flit transmission. It specifies transmitter enable controls, rate hints, and stream identification parameters required to manage protocol-specific flit support in PCI Express architectures.

children.title: Section 7.8.14.4 NOP Flit Control 2 Register
children.description: This section defines the NOP Flit Control 2 Register, detailing bit-level specifications for NOP Stream ID ranges, Debug Opcode requests, and transmitter-initiated NOP.Debug Flit configurations. It addresses how to programmatically trigger NOP flit generation and establish the operational parameters for debug and vendor-specific stream identification.

children.title: Section 7.8.14.5 NOP Flit Status Register
children.description: This section defines the NOP Flit Status Register, detailing register bit assignments for NOP.Debug flit transmission, including Debug Opcodes, flit counts, and Vendor IDs. It specifies register attributes and operational status bits to explain how to trigger, monitor, and configure NOP.Debug flit requests within the transmitter.

children.title: Section 7.9 Additional PCI and PCIe Capabilities
children.description: This section details extended PCI and PCIe capabilities, covering technical specifications for Virtual Channels, Device Serial Numbers, and various vendor-specific or complex-link headers. It answers questions regarding the configuration, management, and architectural standardization of specialized device features, root complex associations, and multicast functionality within a PCIe fabric.

children.title: Section 7.9.1 Virtual Channel Extended Capability
children.description: This section defines the Virtual Channel (VC) Extended Capability for PCI Express devices supporting traffic beyond default TC0/VC0 assignments. It details the structural requirements, register interpretations based on Port type, and the discovery of VC counts through the Extended VC Count field.

children.title: Section 7.9.1.1 Virtual Channel Extended Capability Header (Offset 00h)
children.description: This section details the structure of the PCI Express Virtual Channel (VC) Extended Capability Header, including the PCI Express Extended Capability ID, capability version, and next capability pointer. It defines the header’s bit-field layout to answer how software identifies the VC capability block and navigates the linked list of extended PCIe capabilities.

children.title: Section 7.9.1.2 Port VC Capability Register 1 (Offset 04h)
children.description: This section defines the Virtual Channel Extended Capability Header, detailing the bit-level requirements for the Extended Capability ID (0002h or 0009h), Capability Version, and Next Capability Offset. It functions as a technical specification for parsing PCI Express configuration space linked lists and identifying VC capability structure versions.

children.title: Section 7.9.1.3 Port VC Capability Register 2 (Offset 08h)
children.description: This section defines the **Port VC Capability Register 2**, detailing bit-field layouts for **VC Arbitration Capability** and **VC Arbitration Table Offset** parameters. It specifies how these fields govern **Virtual Channel (VC)** resource management and arbitration mechanisms within the **PCI Express** configuration space.

children.title: Section 7.9.1.4 Port VC Control Register (Offset 0Ch)
children.description: This section defines the **Port VC Capability Register 2**, detailing bit-field specifications for **VC Arbitration Capability** supported by the Function and the **VC Arbitration Table Offset** used to locate the arbitration table in DQWORDS. The text identifies supported arbitration schemes, such as **Hardware Fixed** and various **Weighted Round Robin (WRR)** configurations, to answer how a PCI Express Port manages VC arbitration configuration and table addressing.

children.title: Section 7.9.1.5 Port VC Status Register (Offset 0Eh)
children.description: This section details the **Port VC Control Register** architecture, covering **VC Arbitration Table** management, **VC Arbitration Select** configuration, and the **All VCs Enabled** status bit. It specifies the technical procedures for software-driven **Virtual Channel (VC)** initialization, resource allocation policies, and the hardware-software handshake protocols required to validate VC arbitration updates.

children.title: Section 7.9.1.6 VC Resource Capability Register
children.description: This section defines the **VC Resource Capability Register** within the PCI Express architecture, covering technical specifications for **Port Arbitration**, **Reject Snoop Transactions**, and **Maximum Time Slots**. It addresses how hardware tracks **VC Arbitration Table** coherency and describes the configuration capabilities available for Switch Ports, Root Ports, and RCRBs.

children.title: Section 7.9.1.7 VC Resource Control Register
children.description: This section defines the **VC Resource Control Register** within the PCIe specification, focusing on **Port Arbitration Select** configurations, **Reject Snoop Transactions** settings for Root Ports, and **Maximum Time Slots** capabilities. It provides technical specifications for managing hardware arbitration schemes—such as fixed, WRR, and time-based WRR—and clarifies operational constraints for transaction snooping.

children.title: Section 7.9.1.8 VC Resource Status Register
children.description: This section defines the **VC Resource Status Register** for PCIe Virtual Channels, detailing bit-level functionality for **Port Arbitration Table Status** and **VC Negotiation Pending** states. It specifies requirements for disabling channels and clarifies register applicability across different PCIe device types, such as **RCRBs**, **Root Ports**, and **Switch Ports**.

children.title: Section 7.9.1.10 Port Arbitration Table
children.description: This section details the **VC Arbitration Table** structure, a read-write register array used to implement **WRR (Weighted Round Robin)** arbitration for **Virtual Channels (VC)**. It defines the configuration of **4-bit register entries**—specifically mapping **VC IDs to arbitration phases**—and specifies how the table length is determined and initialized to ensure default VC forward progress.

children.title: Section 7.9.2 Multi-Function Virtual Channel Extended Capability
children.description: This section defines the PCIe Multi-Function Virtual Channel (MFVC) Extended Capability, covering register definitions for VC resource management, arbitration mechanisms, and port control. It details how multi-function devices manage traffic prioritization and resource allocation, answering questions regarding the programming interface for VC Arbitration Tables and Function Arbitration Tables.

children.title: Section 7.9.2.1 MFVC Extended Capability Header (Offset 00h)
children.description: This section defines the Multi-Function Virtual Channel (MFVC) Extended Capability structure, covering requirements for Function 0 deployment, Port VC Capability Register integration, and QoS management for multi-function devices. It addresses device implementation rules for VC/TC mapping, arbitration policies, and compatibility constraints regarding the SIOV Extended Capability.

children.title: Section 7.9.2.2 MFVC Port VC Capability Register 1 (Offset 04h)
children.description: This section defines the MFVC Port VC Capability Register 1, detailing the structure of the PCI Express Extended Capability Header, including the Capability ID (0008h), versioning, and linked list offsets. It explains how to interpret register fields for Multi-Function Device Virtual Channel configuration, specifically addressing the Extended VC Count and Low Priority Extended VC Count parameters.

children.title: Section 7.9.2.3 MFVC Port VC Capability Register 2 (Offset 08h)
children.description: This section defines the **MFVC Port VC Capability Register 2**, detailing technical configurations for **Extended VC Count**, **Low Priority Extended VC Count**, **Reference Clock** intervals, and **Function Arbitration Table Entry Size**. It specifies hardware capabilities for virtual channel management, establishing how many virtual channels a device supports and the parameters required for time-based WRR and arbitration operations.

children.title: Section 7.9.2.4 MFVC Port VC Control Register (Offset 0Ch)
children.description: This section defines the **MFVC Port VC Control** and **Capability Register 2** fields, detailing technical specifications for **VC Arbitration** schemes, **WRR phase configurations**, and **table offset mapping**. It answers how to configure low-priority virtual channel (LPVC) arbitration and how to locate the MFVC arbitration table within the Extended Capability structure.

children.title: Section 7.9.2.5 MFVC Port VC Status Register (Offset 0Eh)
children.description: This section details the **MFVC Port VC Status Register**, covering **hardware flow control**, **VC negotiation status**, and **buffer throughput monitoring** within the **Multi-Function Virtual Channel (MFVC)** architecture. It defines the register’s bit-level field definitions to address questions regarding current **negotiation completion states**, **error reporting**, and **operational readiness** of virtual channels.

children.title: Section 7.9.2.6 MFVC VC Resource Capability Register
children.description: This section defines the **MFVC VC Resource Capability Register** and **MFVC Port VC Status Register**, covering **VC Arbitration Select** configurations and the **All VCs Enabled** hardware resource allocation bit. It specifies functional constraints for **LPVC group** management and operational status reporting to ensure correct initialization of **Virtual Channels** within a **Multi-Function Device** PCIe architecture.

children.title: Section 7.9.2.7 MFVC VC Resource Control Register
children.description: This section defines the MFVC VC Resource Capability Register, detailing Function Arbitration capabilities—including hardware-fixed, WRR, and time-based arbitration schemes—and the Maximum Time Slots parameter. It specifies the configuration bitfields and attributes required for software to select and manage arbitration services within a Virtual Channel resource.

children.title: Section 7.9.2.8 MFVC VC Resource Status Register
children.description: This section details the **MFVC VC Resource Status Register**, specifically covering **Flow Control (FC)** buffer allocation, **Virtual Channel (VC)** availability, and status-monitoring flags. It defines the operational state of memory resources to answer how hardware tracks buffer capacity and manages congestion within multi-function virtual channel architectures.

children.title: Section 7.9.2.9 MFVC VC Arbitration Table
children.description: This section defines the **MFVC VC Resource Status Register**, detailing the **Function Arbitration Table Status** and **VC Negotiation Pending** bit functions. It specifies the hardware-software interaction protocols for updating arbitration tables and managing Virtual Channel initialization states within the PCIe architecture.

children.title: Section 7.9.2.10 Function Arbitration Table
children.description: This section defines the **Function Arbitration Table** register within the **MFVC Extended Capability** structure, detailing its role in implementing **WRR or time-based WRR arbitration** for **Multi-Function Devices**. It specifies technical requirements for **table entry sizing** based on **ARI support** and the presence of **Function Groups**, answering how programmable arbitration periods are configured and stored for VC resources.

children.title: Section 7.9.3 Device Serial Number Extended Capability
children.description: This section defines the Device Serial Number Extended Capability for PCI Express devices, specifying its 64-bit read-only structure and implementation requirements for Multi-Function Devices and RCiEPs. It clarifies mandatory function-zero implementation and reporting consistency, while simultaneously providing reference data on Function Arbitration Table lengths.

children.title: Section 7.9.3.1 Device Serial Number Extended Capability Header (Offset 00h)
children.description: This section specifies the layout and field definitions for the PCIe Device Serial Number Extended Capability (Extended Capability ID 0003h), including its header structure, versioning, and offset pointer. It addresses how to identify, locate, and structure the 64-bit unique device serial number within the PCI Express configuration space.

children.title: Section 7.9.3.2 Serial Number Register (Offset 04h)
children.description: This section defines the 64-bit PCI Express Device Serial Number register, detailing its mapping to the IEEE EUI-64 standard, including the 24-bit company ID and 40-bit manufacturer extension. It identifies the bit-level register structure and RO (Read-Only) attributes necessary for hardware identification and vendor-specific capability implementation.

children.title: Section 7.9.4 Vendor-Specific Capability
children.description: This section details the 64-bit PCI Express Device Serial Number register, specifying its EUI-64 format and byte-endianness mapping. It also introduces the Vendor-Specific Capability structure, providing the regulatory framework for vendors to implement custom configuration space headers.

children.title: Section 7.9.5 Vendor-Specific Extended Capability
children.description: This section details the Vendor-Specific Extended Capability (VSEC), formalizing the structure of the 09h Capability ID, Next Capability Pointer, and Capability Length fields. It defines how PCI Express Functions and RCRBs can implement custom register blocks to expose vendor-specific features and hardware metadata.

children.title: Section 7.9.5.1 Vendor-Specific Extended Capability Header (Offset 00h)
children.description: This section defines the structure and bit-level layout of the PCI Express Vendor-Specific Extended Capability (VSEC) Header, identified by Extended Capability ID 000Bh. It provides technical specifications for the Capability Version and Next Capability Offset fields, enabling developers to determine how to navigate the linked list of PCIe extended capability structures.

children.title: Section 7.9.5.2 Vendor-Specific Header (Offset 04h)
children.description: This section defines the 32-bit Vendor-Specific Header structure, detailing fields for VSEC ID, VSEC Rev, and VSEC Length within the PCI Express Extended Capability architecture. It clarifies how software must qualify Vendor IDs to interpret vendor-specific registers and introduces the optional Designated Vendor-Specific Extended Capability (DVSEC).

children.title: Section 7.9.6 Designated Vendor-Specific Extended Capability (DVSEC)
children.description: This section defines the Designated Vendor-Specific Extended Capability (DVSEC) header structure, covering register bit definitions for VSEC ID, VSEC Rev, and VSEC Length. It specifies mandatory software qualification protocols—validating Vendor ID and VSEC ID before interpretation—to ensure interoperability when exposing vendor-specific registers in PCI Express Functions or RCRBs.

children.title: Section 7.9.6.1 Designated Vendor-Specific Extended Capability Header (Offset 00h)
children.description: This section defines the structure and field definitions for the Designated Vendor-Specific Extended Capability (DVSEC) header, utilizing Extended Capability ID 0023h. It specifies the configuration layout for the header’s PCI Express Extended Capability ID, versioning, and offset fields while outlining the register architecture for vendor-specific implementations.

children.title: Section 7.9.6.2 Designated Vendor-Specific Header 1 (Offset 04h)
children.description: This section defines the Designated Vendor-Specific Header 1 (Offset 04h) within the PCIe configuration space, detailing its 32-bit register structure and bit-field allocations. It addresses how vendors implement proprietary capabilities, standard header formatting, and the mandatory field mapping necessary for device vendor-specific identification and compliance.

children.title: Section 7.9.6.3 Designated Vendor-Specific Header 2 (Offset 08h)
children.description: This section defines the Designated Vendor-Specific Extended Capability (DVSEC) structure, detailing the register bit-fields for Header 1 and Header 2. It clarifies the requirements for software to validate Vendor IDs and DVSEC IDs before interpreting revision levels or structure lengths.

children.title: Section 7.9.7 RCRB Header Extended Capability
children.description: This section defines the Root Complex Register Block (RCRB) Header Extended Capability implemented within PCI Express architectures. It details register-level specifications, including Vendor/Device IDs, capability pointers, and control mechanisms, to answer how system software identifies and configures RCRB-based Root Complex components.

children.title: Section 7.9.7.1 RCRB Header Extended Capability Header (Offset 00h)
children.description: This section defines the RCRB Header Extended Capability (ID 000Ah), detailing its register map and structural configuration for Root Complex management. It answers questions regarding the layout of the RCRB Header, the purpose of vendor-specific identification, and the specific offsets for capability, control, and status registers within the PCI Express architecture.

children.title: Section 7.9.7.2 RCRB Vendor ID and Device ID register (Offset 04h)
children.description: This section defines the 16-bit Vendor ID and 16-bit Device ID fields within the Root Complex Register Block (RCRB), identifying the manufacturer and specific hardware implementation for PCI Express Root Complex components. It informs developers how the host software authenticates and configures internal root complex logic during system enumeration and initialization.

children.title: Section 7.9.7.3 RCRB Capabilities register (Offset 08h)
children.description: This section defines the RCRB Header Extended Capability and RCRB Vendor/Device ID registers, detailing field attributes for PCIe Extended Capability IDs, versioning, and linked-list offsets. It addresses how to identify RCRB structure parameters and vendor-specific device information within PCI Express configuration space.

children.title: Section 7.9.7.4 RCRB Control register (Offset 0Ch)
children.description: This section defines the RCRB Control and Capabilities registers, specifically detailing the Configuration Request Retry Status (RRS) Software Visibility bit. It addresses how to programmatically enable or identify Root Complex support for RRS Completion Status across associated Root Ports and integrated devices.

children.title: Section 7.9.8 Root Complex Link Declaration Extended Capability
children.description: This section defines the Root Complex Link Declaration Extended Capability, detailing the Configuration RRS Software Visibility and Enable registers used to manage Request Retry Status (RRS) within Root Complex Register Blocks (RCRBs). It answers how to implement topology declaration and enable retry completion status for Root Ports and integrated devices.

children.title: Section 7.9.8.1 Root Complex Link Declaration Extended Capability Header (Offset 00h)
children.description: This section defines the **Root Complex Link Declaration Extended Capability Header**, specifying the structure, **Extended Capability ID (0005h)**, and **Next Capability Offset** fields for PCI Express configuration. It provides the technical layout for identifying and navigating link entries within the Root Complex architecture in compliance with the **PCI Express Base Specification**.

children.title: Section 7.9.8.2 Element Self Description Register (Offset 04h)
children.description: This section defines the Element Self Description Register (Offset 04h) within the Root Complex Link Declaration Extended Capability, detailing field encodings for Element Type, Component ID, and Port Number. It provides the technical specifications necessary to identify Root Complex elements and establish the linked list structure of PCI Express Extended Capabilities.

children.title: Section 7.9.8.3 Link Entries
children.description: This section details the architectural specification for Link Entries, covering the Link Description Register and the dual-format addressing schemes required for Link Types 0 and 1. It addresses technical requirements for memory-mapped structural linkage, specifically defining how hardware pointers must be formatted and interpreted during system enumeration or data traversal.

children.title: Section 7.9.8.3.1 Link Description Register
children.description: This section defines the **Link Description Register** and the structure of **Link Entries** within the **Root Complex Link Declaration Extended Capability**. It details bit-level field definitions—including **Link Valid**, **Link Type**, and **Link Address**—to specify how the system identifies and validates internal links between **Root Complex Elements** and **RCRBs**.

children.title: Section 7.9.8.3.2.1 Link Address for Link Type 0
children.description: This section defines the mandatory address formatting and field structures for Link Type 0 within a networked architecture. It specifies hardware-level addressing requirements, transmission protocols, and parameter constraints to resolve how nodes establish and validate point-to-point communication links.

children.title: Section 7.9.8.3.2.2 Link Address for Link Type 1
children.description: This section defines the configuration parameters for Link Type 1 entries within a Root Complex, specifically detailing the `Associate RCRB Header`, `Target Component ID`, and `Target Port Number` bit fields. It specifies how these registers identify the target Configuration Space address for PCI Express Root Ports or RCiEPs, effectively answering how software maps and locates targeted elements within a Root Complex hierarchy.

children.title: Section 7.9.9 Root Complex Internal Link Control Extended Capability
children.description: This section defines the architectural structure of the Root Complex Internal Link Control Extended Capability, focusing on the configuration space base address, bus-device-function (BDF) routing, and the variable-width field $n$ that supports multiple PCI segment groups. It explains how to interpret the register bit-fields to determine memory-mapped configuration space addresses for interconnecting Root Complex components.

children.title: Section 7.9.9.1 Root Complex Internal Link Control Extended Capability Header (Offset 00h)
children.description: This section defines the PCIe Extended Capability Header structure for the Root Complex Internal Link Control, specifying fields for Capability ID, Capability Version, and Next Capability Offset. It details the register layout required for identifying and navigating the Root Complex Internal Link Control capability block within the configuration space.

children.title: Section 7.9.9.2 Root Complex Link Capabilities Register (Offset 04h)
children.description: This section details the Root Complex Internal Link Control Extended Capability, specifying the structure of its header, including the 0006h capability ID, versioning, and linked-list traversal via the Next Capability Offset. It identifies the configuration space register layout, defining how software identifies and navigates the Root Complex's internal link control mechanisms within the PCI Express architecture.

children.title: Section 7.9.9.3 Root Complex Link Control Register (Offset 08h)
children.description: This section details the Root Complex Link Control Register (Offset 08h), focusing on Link speed configuration bits for 16.0, 32.0, and 64.0 GT/s operations. It provides technical guidelines for software-based link speed management, explaining how to interpret backward-compatible speed encodings and properly utilize the Supported Link Speeds Vector for current and future PCIe hardware.

children.title: Section 7.9.9.4 Root Complex Link Status Register (Offset 0Ah)
children.description: This section defines the Root Complex Link Status Register, detailing how Current Link Speed and Negotiated Link Width are reported for PCI Express links. It clarifies link training parameters, encoding definitions for speed and width, and functional requirements for Root Complex status reporting.

children.title: Section 7.9.10 Root Complex Event Collector Endpoint Association Extended Capability
children.description: This section defines the configuration structure and register mapping for the Root Complex Event Collector (RCEC) Extended Capability, specifically focusing on the association between the RCEC and associated Root Complex Integrated Endpoints (RCiEPs). It details how to interpret the Association Bitmap and Associated Bus Numbers registers to resolve interrupt and error routing logic between the Root Complex and its constituent endpoints.

children.title: Section 7.9.10.1 Root Complex Event Collector Endpoint Association Extended Capability Header (Offset 00h)
children.description: This section defines the **Root Complex Event Collector (RCEC) Endpoint Association Extended Capability** header structure (ID 0007h), which uses a **DWORD association bitmap** and **bus number ranges** to identify associated **RCiEPs**. It specifies implementation requirements for **Root Complex Event Collectors** and differentiates associated **PCI Express** functions from non-associated devices like Root Ports.

children.title: Section 7.9.10.2 Association Bitmap for RCiEPs (Offset 04h)
children.description: This section defines the Association Bitmap register within the Root Complex Integrated Endpoint (RCiEP) Association Extended Capability, detailing how bit-vector mapping establishes logical associations between RCiEPs and Root Ports. Technical content specifies architectural binding mechanisms for platform software, answering how hardware identifies specific RCiEP dependencies and organizational relationships within the PCIe fabric hierarchy.

children.title: Section 7.9.10.3 RCEC Associated Bus Numbers Register (Offset 08h)
children.description: This section defines the RCEC Associated Bus Numbers and RCiEP Association Bitmap registers, covering specifications for the Root Complex Event Collector Extended Capability (ID 0007h). It specifies configuration space offsets, versioning requirements, and register bit-fields to explain how RCiEPs are associated with their corresponding Root Complex Event Collector within the PCI Express architecture.

children.title: Section 7.9.11 Multicast Extended Capability
children.description: This section defines the architectural requirements and register specifications for the PCI Express **Multicast Extended Capability**, detailing its applicability to **Root Ports, Switches, Endpoints, SR-IOV VFs, and SIOV SDIs**. It specifically addresses how to configure **RCEC Last Bus** fields and mandates consistency across multiple capability structures within a single physical component.

children.title: Section 7.9.11.1 Multicast Extended Capability Header (Offset 00h)
children.description: This section details the PCIe Multicast Extended Capability Header, defining the structure and bit-level specifications—including Capability ID, Version, and Next Capability Offset—required for identifying and traversing the Multicast Extended Capability. It answers technical questions regarding the architectural layout of the Multicast capability structure and the specific field assignments necessary for PCI Express Extended Capability interoperability.

children.title: Section 7.9.11.2 Multicast Capability Register (Offset 04h)
children.description: This section specifies the Multicast Capability Register, detailing header fields (Capability ID, Version, Next Offset) and structural parameters including `MC_Max_Group`, `MC_Window_Size_Requested`, and `MC_ECRC_Regeneration_Supported`. It defines the component's multicast hardware limits, support for ECRC regeneration, and identifies Reserved-as-Preserved (RsvdP) constraints for Virtual Functions (VFs).

children.title: Section 7.9.11.3 Multicast Control Register (Offset 06h)
children.description: This section details the Multicast Control Register (Offset 06h), defining bit-level configurations for packet filtering, group membership management, and hardware-based traffic forwarding protocols. It answers how multicast address filtering is enabled and how the data link layer manages targeted transmission thresholds within the device.

children.title: Section 7.9.11.4 MC_Base_Address Register (Offset 08h)
children.description: This section defines the configuration parameters for multicast group management, specifically detailing the `MC_Base_Address` register and the `Multicast Control` register. It explains how to set the number of multicast groups and determine their memory address mapping via `MC_Index_Position` and `MC_Base_Address` fields.

children.title: Section 7.9.11.5 MC_Receive Register (Offset 10h)
children.description: This section defines the **MC_Receive Register (Offset 10h)**, detailing its bit-field configuration, data handling protocols, and operational constraints for incoming **message processing**. It addresses how to initialize peripheral communication, interpret status flags, and manage buffer thresholds during **data reception sequences**.

children.title: Section 7.9.11.6 MC_Block_All Register (Offset 18h)
children.description: This section defines the **MC_Block_All** and **MC_Receive** registers within the **Multicast (MC) Capability** structure, specifying bit vector configurations for controlling **Multicast TLP routing** and **Group filtering**. It details register offsets, field attributes, and hardware requirements for managing **MC_Base_Address** and **MC_Max_Group** parameters to determine functional multicast reception and blocking behavior.

children.title: Section 7.9.11.7 MC_Block_Untranslated Register (Offset 20h)
children.description: This section details the **MC_Block_Untranslated register** at offset 20h, covering memory controller translation bypass, block-level address mapping, and fault-handling protocols. It answers how to configure untranslated memory access, identify specific block status indicators, and manage hardware privilege levels during block virtualization.

children.title: Section 7.9.11.8 MC_Overlay_BAR Register (Offset 28h)
children.description: This section defines the control mechanisms for PCIe Multicast (MC) functionality, specifically addressing the `MC_Block_All` and `MC_Block_Untranslated` registers alongside the `MC_Overlay_BAR` requirements. It clarifies how hardware manages TLP routing restrictions for specific Multicast Groups and identifies which PCIe device types (Switches, Root Complex Ports, or Endpoints) must implement these registers to ensure compliant Multicast address handling.

children.title: Section 7.9.12 Dynamic Power Allocation Extended Capability (DPA Capability)
children.description: This section defines the PCIe Dynamic Power Allocation (DPA) Extended Capability structure, including the MC_Overlay_BAR register for Multicast TLP windowing and the DPA-specific registers for latency, status, and control. It addresses how hardware managed overlay apertures are configured and provides the functional layout for managing power allocation states within the extended capability memory map.

children.title: Section 7.9.12.1 DPA Extended Capability Header (Offset 00h)
children.description: This section defines the DPA Extended Capability Header (Offset 00h) within the PCIe configuration space, detailing the Capability ID, Version, and Next Capability Pointer fields. It clarifies how software determines the DPA (Device Power Allocation) structure’s size, versioning, and link to subsequent extended capabilities.

children.title: Section 7.9.12.2 DPA Capability Register (Offset 04h)
children.description: This section defines the DPA (Dynamic Power Allocation) Capability Register structure, detailing field-level specifications for the PCI Express Extended Capability Header and DPA Capability register. It provides technical definitions for parameters including the Substate_Max, transition latency, and power allocation scale, addressing how a device reports its specific power management constraints and substate capacities.

children.title: Section 7.9.12.3 DPA Latency Indicator Register (Offset 08h)
children.description: This section defines the DPA Latency Indicator Register, detailing Transition Latency Value (Xlcy) fields, Transition Latency Unit (Tlunit) scaling, and Power Allocation Scale (PAS) factors. It specifies how these parameters map to individual device substates to determine maximum transition latency and power allocation requirements.

children.title: Section 7.9.12.4 DPA Status Register (Offset 0Ch)
children.description: This section defines the DPA Status Register (Offset 0Ch) bitfields, providing diagnostic feedback on device power management states and operational integrity. It answers queries regarding current power-delivery status, hardware state transitions, and error-reporting mechanisms within the DPA architecture.

children.title: Section 7.9.12.5 DPA Control Register (Offset 0Eh)
children.description: This section defines the DPA (Dynamic Power Allocation) Status and Control registers, detailing how software manages and monitors PCIe function substates via the Substate Status, Substate Control, and Substate Control Enabled bits. It addresses how to initiate substate transitions and explains the hardware-software interaction required to enable or disable substate control functionality.

children.title: Section 7.9.12.6 DPA Power Allocation Array
children.description: This section defines the DPA Power Allocation Array, detailing the configuration of Substate Power Allocation registers used to calculate function-specific power requirements via a scaling factor. It explains how these registers map to substates and addresses requirements for TPH Requester Extended Capability implementation across Physical and Virtual Functions.

children.title: Section 7.9.13 TPH Requester Extended Capability
children.description: This section defines the TPH Requester Extended Capability structure, detailing the configuration of Substate Power Allocation registers and their associated scaling factors for PCIe functions. It specifies implementation requirements for Multi-Function Devices, Physical Functions (PFs), and Virtual Functions (VFs), answering how power substate allocation is registered and standardized across associated VFs.

children.title: Section 7.9.13.1 TPH Requester Extended Capability Header (Offset 00h)
children.description: This section defines the **PCIe TPH (Transaction Layer Packet Processing Hints) Requester Extended Capability Header**, detailing register fields for **Capability ID**, **Next Capability Offset**, and **Capability Version**. It specifies the structural configuration required to identify and navigate the TPH requester mechanism within the **PCI Express Extended Configuration Space**.

children.title: Section 7.9.13.2 TPH Requester Capability Register (Offset 04h)
children.description: This section defines the TPH Requester Capability Register and Extended Capability Header, detailing the architectural layout and field specifications for TPH Requester functionality. It addresses how to identify TPH capabilities, determine supported steering tag (ST) modes, and configure ST table parameters within the PCIe Extended Capability structure.

children.title: Section 7.9.13.3 TPH Requester Control Register (Offset 08h)
children.description: This section defines the TPH Requester Capability Register bit-fields, detailing hardware support for No ST, Interrupt Vector, and Device Specific steering tag modes. It answers how a system identifies ST table locations, table capacity via the ST Table Size field, and whether a function supports Extended TPH TLP prefixes.

children.title: Section 7.9.13.4 TPH ST Table (Starting from Offset 0Ch)
children.description: This section defines the TPH Requester Control register and TPH ST Table structure, detailing ST Mode Select and TPH Requester Enable settings. It specifies configuration requirements for issuing TPH and Extended TPH Request TLPs and outlines the memory-mapped layout for ST Upper and Lower Table entries.

children.title: Section 7.9.14 DPC Extended Capability
children.description: Section 7.9.14 defines the Downstream Port Containment (DPC) Extended Capability, outlining technical requirements for error containment, DL_Active reporting, and optional ERR_COR Subclass support in Root Ports and Switch Downstream Ports. This section addresses how PCIe hardware handles uncorrectable errors and specifies the integration criteria for ports implementing DPC and RP Extensions.

children.title: Section 7.9.14.1 DPC Extended Capability Header (Offset 00h)
children.description: This section defines the DPC Extended Capability Header, detailing mandatory PCI Express capability ID, version, and next-capability pointer fields. It addresses how system software identifies the DPC structure and determines its version compliance for managing downstream port containment errors.

children.title: Section 7.9.14.2 DPC Capability Register (Offset 04h)
children.description: This section defines the DPC Capability Register structure, detailing fields for interrupt messaging, Root Port extensions, TLP blocking, and software triggering. It addresses how the DPC Extended Capability header and associated register bits characterize Downstream Port Containment features and log sizes in PCI Express devices.

children.title: Section 7.9.14.3 DPC Control Register (Offset 06h)
children.description: Section 7.9.14.3 details the DPC Control Register configuration for PCIe Downstream Port Containment. It specifies bit-level settings for trigger criteria, completion status (CA/UR), interrupt enablement, and error reporting, answering how to administratively enable and gate DPC event handling.

children.title: Section 7.9.14.4 DPC Status Register (Offset 08h)
children.description: This section defines the DPC Status Register (Offset 08h), detailing the functional bits for triggering events, RP busy conditions, and error pointers within a PCIe Downstream Port Containment (DPC) architecture. It specifies hardware behavior for LTSSM transitions during error states and establishes the procedural requirements for software to clear trigger flags and recover from DPC events.

children.title: Section 7.9.14.5 DPC Error Source ID Register (Offset 0Ah)
children.description: This section defines the DPC Error Source ID register, detailing the "DPC RP Busy" status flag, "DPC Trigger Reason Extension" encodings, and the "RP PIO First Error Pointer" for root port diagnostics. It specifies register behavior, bit-field attributes, and the conditions required for clearing DPC status triggers in root ports supporting RP Extensions.

children.title: Section 7.9.14.6 RP PIO Status Register (Offset 0Ch)
children.description: This section details the **RP PIO Status Register** (offset 0Ch) and the **DPC Error Source ID Register**, covering the bit-level reporting of **Configuration, I/O, and Memory Request errors** like **UR Completion**, **CA Completion**, and **Completion Timeout (CTO)**. It specifies how Root Ports identify the **Requester ID** of DPC-triggering error messages and explains the status register’s **RW1CS** attributes for fault tracking.

children.title: Section 7.9.14.7 RP PIO Mask Register (Offset 10h)
children.description: This section details the Root Port (RP) PIO Mask Register, covering bit-level configurations for masking Programmed Input/Output (PIO) events and hardware error reporting. It addresses how to filter specific interrupt sources and control synchronization between the root complex and subordinate devices during register-level access.

children.title: Section 7.9.14.8 RP PIO Severity Register (Offset 14h)
children.description: This section defines the **RP PIO Severity Register** (offset 14h), which configures error reporting severity levels for **PCI Express Root Ports** supporting **RP Extensions for DPC**. It explains how to programmatically control whether specific **Configuration**, **I/O**, and **Memory Request** completion errors—such as **UR (Unsupported Request)**, **CA (Completer Abort)**, and **Completion Timeout (CTO)**—are treated as fatal or non-fatal events.

children.title: Section 7.9.14.9 RP PIO SysError Register (Offset 18h)
children.description: This section defines the **RP PIO SysError Register** (Offset 18h), which configures error severity for Root Ports supporting **DPC (Downstream Port Containment)** extensions. It details the bit-level mapping for **Configuration, I/O, and Memory transaction failures**, including **Unsupported Requests (UR)**, **Completer Aborts (CA)**, and **Completion Timeouts (CTO)**.

children.title: Section 7.9.14.10 RP PIO Exception Register (Offset 1Ch)
children.description: This section defines the **RP PIO Exception Register**, specifying bit-field assignments for tracking **Configuration, I/O, and Memory transaction errors** such as **UR (Unsupported Request), CA (Completer Abort), and CTO (Completion Timeout)**. It details register attributes and default states for Root Ports supporting **RP Extensions for DPC**, identifying how systems log and categorize PIO-related exception events within the PCIe architecture.

children.title: Section 7.9.14.11 RP PIO Header Log Register (Offset 20h)
children.description: This section defines the **RP PIO Header Log Register**, which captures the **Request TLP header** associated with **root port PIO errors** such as **UR completions**, **CA completions**, and **completion timeouts (CTO)**. It details the register’s memory layout, specific bit-field attributes for Configuration, I/O, and Memory requests, and handling variations between **Non-Flit and Flit Mode** implementations.

children.title: Section 7.9.14.12 RP PIO ImpSpec Log Register (Offset 30h)
children.description: This section defines the **RP PIO Implementer-Specific (ImpSpec) Log Register**, detailing its **Memory-Mapped I/O (MMIO) offset (30h)**, field bit-definitions, and hardware error-logging functionality within the **PCI Express Root Port architecture**. It specifies how system software tracks vendor-defined diagnostic data and fault triggers to facilitate advanced reliability, availability, and serviceability (RAS) reporting.

children.title: Section 7.9.14.13 RP PIO TLP Prefix Log Register (Offset 34h)
children.description: This section defines the **RP PIO TLP Prefix Log Register** and **RP PIO ImpSpec Log Register**, detailing how Root Ports supporting **DPC (Downstream Port Containment)** log TLP headers, End-to-End TLP Prefixes, and implementation-specific error metadata. It specifies register bit layouts, access attributes, and implementation requirements for capturing diagnostic information during PIO error events.

children.title: Section 7.9.15 Precision Time Measurement Extended Capability (PTM Extended Capability)
children.description: This section specifies the implementation requirements and register sizing for the Root Port PIO TLP Prefix Log within the Precision Time Measurement (PTM) Extended Capability. It answers how to calculate allocated DWORD size, map End-End TLP Prefixes to log registers, and handle cases where Root Ports do not transmit targeted Non-Posted Requests.

children.title: Section 7.9.15.1 PTM Extended Capability Header (Offset 00h)
children.description: This section defines the PTM (Precision Time Measurement) Extended Capability Header structure, detailing specific PCI Express configuration space fields like the PTM Capability ID, Version, and Next Capability Offset. It addresses how PTM-capable devices implement discovery and link-layer timing synchronization protocols within the PCIe extended configuration space.

children.title: Section 7.9.15.2 PTM Capability Register (Offset 04h)
children.description: This section defines the PTM Capability Register and the PTM Extended Capability Header, which structure Precision Time Measurement support in PCI Express devices. It specifies register bit layouts, including IDs, versioning, and linked-list offset pointers, to answer how a Function identifies and configures PTM capabilities.

children.title: Section 7.9.15.3 PTM Control Register (Offset 08h)
children.description: This section defines the PCIe PTM Control Register, detailing bit assignments for PTM Enable, Root Select, and Effective Granularity. It clarifies how to configure a Function's participation in Precision Time Measurement, specifying the roles of PTM Roots versus requesters within a PTM Hierarchy.

children.title: Section 7.9.16 Readiness Time Reporting Extended Capability
children.description: This section details the Readiness Time Reporting Extended Capability, covering Configuration-Ready timing specifications, PTM granularity, and mandatory VF implementation rules. It defines the hierarchical criteria software must use to determine when a device is ready to process requests, effectively optimizing initialization delays.

children.title: Section 7.9.16.1 Readiness Time Reporting Extended Capability Header (Offset 00h)
children.description: This section defines the Readiness Time Reporting Extended Capability, detailing register structures and floating-point time encoding ($Value \times Multiplier[Scale]$) for PCIe devices. It explains how system software manages timing reporting, the use of the "Valid" status bit, and the method for calculating device initialization readiness intervals.

children.title: Section 7.9.16.2 Readiness Time Reporting 1 Register (Offset 04h)
children.description: This section details the Readiness Time Reporting 1 Register structure (Offset 04h) within the Readiness Time Reporting Extended Capability, defining its PCI Express Extended Capability header fields and specific bit-mapped timing attributes. It specifies the configuration for Reset Time, DL_Up Time, and Valid status bits, providing the technical requirements for hardware readiness reporting in PCI-compatible Configuration Space.

children.title: Section 7.9.16.3 Readiness Time Reporting 2 Register (Offset 08h)
children.description: This section defines the Readiness Time Reporting 2 Register, detailing fields for FLR Time, D3Hot to D0 Time, and DL_Up Time. It outlines hardware initialization requirements and validity conditions to help system software determine the duration a PCI Express Function needs to become Configuration-Ready following specific power state transitions or resets.

children.title: Section 7.9.17 Hierarchy ID Extended Capability
children.description: Section 7.9.17 defines the PCI Express Hierarchy ID Extended Capability, detailing technical requirements for hardware implementations across Upstream and Downstream Ports. It specifies configuration rules for Message Requester IDs, System GUIDs, and software-programmed values, answering questions regarding Function-level support, VF/PF synchronization, and compliance mandates for Hierarchy ID Message usage.

children.title: Section 7.9.17.1 Hierarchy ID Extended Capability Header (Offset 00h)
children.description: This section defines the structure and field specifications for the PCIe Hierarchy ID Extended Capability header, including its Extended Capability ID (0028h), capability versioning, and linked list offset. It answers how software identifies the Hierarchy ID capability and locates subsequent Extended Capability structures within the PCI configuration space.

children.title: Section 7.9.17.2 Hierarchy ID Status Register (Offset 04h)
children.description: This section defines the Hierarchy ID Status Register, covering field specifications for the Message Requester ID, Hierarchy ID Writeable status, and associated configuration attributes. It addresses how hardware distinguishes Hierarchy ID provenance and authority within Upstream Ports, Downstream Ports, and Virtual Functions (VFs).

children.title: Section 7.9.17.3 Hierarchy ID Data Register (Offset 08h)
children.description: This section defines the **Hierarchy ID Data Register (offset 08h)** within the PCIe architecture, covering bit-level specifications for the **Hierarchy ID Valid** status, **System GUID Authority ID**, and the **Hierarchy ID** field. It details the register’s read/write attributes, default states for Downstream Ports, PFs, VFs, and RCiEPs, and explains how software interacts with these fields based on the **Hierarchy ID Writeable** configuration.

children.title: Section 7.9.17.4 Hierarchy ID GUID 1 Register (Offset 0Ch)
children.description: This section defines the **Hierarchy ID GUID 1 Register (Offset 0Ch)**, detailing its bit-field architecture, unique **GUID** identification, and configuration requirements for **PCIe hierarchy** management. It addresses how to program the register to specify system topology and protocol-level mapping within the defined **PCI Configuration Space**.

children.title: Section 7.9.17.5 Hierarchy ID GUID 2 Register (Offset 10h)
children.description: This section defines the Hierarchy ID GUID 1 and 2 registers, detailing their bit-field mappings for bits [143:96] of the System GUID within Hierarchy ID Messages. It specifies the configuration logic, register attributes (RO/RW), and software-programmability requirements based on the Hierarchy ID Writeable status bit.

children.title: Section 7.9.17.6 Hierarchy ID GUID 3 Register (Offset 14h)
children.description: This section defines the **Hierarchy ID GUID 3 Register** at offset **14h**, specifying its bit-field architecture and functional role within the **PCIe configuration space**. It addresses the identification and logical mapping of **system-level hierarchy topologies**, detailing how the register facilitates **Root Complex** routing and **GUID-based resource enumeration**.

children.title: Section 7.9.17.7 Hierarchy ID GUID 4 Register (Offset 18h)
children.description: This section defines the Hierarchy ID GUID 4 Register (Offset 18h), specifying the bit allocation for bits [63:32] of the System GUID field. It clarifies register access attributes (RO/RW), hardware-software initialization rules, and conditions for validity based on the Hierarchy ID Message.

children.title: Section 7.9.17.8 Hierarchy ID GUID 5 Register (Offset 1Ch)
children.description: This section defines the Hierarchy ID GUID 5 Register (Offset 1Ch), specifying its bit-level structure, read/write attributes, and software-defined System GUID mapping. It addresses how hardware identifies system components through Vital Product Data (VPD) and clarifies the register’s operational dependency on Hierarchy ID status.

children.title: Section 7.9.18 Vital Product Data Capability (VPD Capability)
children.description: This section defines the **Vital Product Data (VPD) Capability** structure, focusing on the **Hierarchy ID GUID 5 Register** allocation and read/write attributes for **PCIe Functions**, including **PFs and VFs**. It specifies how system-level hardware identification—such as serial and part numbers—is stored and accessed to support device inventory and serviceability requirements.

children.title: Section 7.9.18.1 VPD Address Register
children.description: This section defines the architectural requirements for the PCI Vital Product Data (VPD) Capability structure, specifically detailing the function of the VPD Address and VPD Data registers. It explains the procedural handshaking protocols using the flag (F) bit to manage asynchronous read and write operations between host software and the VPD storage component.

children.title: Section 7.9.18.2 VPD Data Register
children.description: This section defines the Vital Product Data (VPD) Address and Data registers used to access hardware configuration information via DWORD-aligned memory operations. It details the bit-level specifications for addressing, the "F" flag for read/write synchronization, and the mandatory 32-bit access requirements for hardware compatibility.

children.title: Section 7.9.19 Native PCIe Enclosure Management Extended Capability (NPEM Extended Capability)
children.description: This section details the Native PCIe Enclosure Management (NPEM) Extended Capability, specifically defining the VPD Address and VPD Data registers used for enclosure-based data transfers. It specifies register bit layouts, read/write attributes, and functional requirements for executing VPD read/write operations within a PCIe component.

children.title: Section 7.9.19.1 NPEM Extended Capability Header (Offset 00h)
children.description: This section defines the mandatory PCI Express Extended Capability Header for the Native PCIe Enclosure Management (NPEM) capability, specifying the structure for the Capability ID, Version, and Next Capability Pointer. It addresses the fundamental requirements for identifying and navigating the NPEM configuration space within the PCIe extended capability list.

children.title: Section 7.9.19.2 NPEM Capability Register (Offset 04h)
children.description: This section defines the NPEM (Native PCIe Enclosure Management) Extended Capability register structure, detailing the header fields, versioning, and required support for mandatory and optional enclosure states. It answers technical questions regarding the identification of the 0029h Capability ID, the layout of the NPEM Capability Register (offset 04h), and the compliance requirements for OK, Locate, Fail, and Rebuild indicator states.

children.title: Section 7.9.19.3 NPEM Control Register (Offset 08h)
children.description: This section defines the NPEM Control Register (offset 08h), focusing on the operational state of enclosure management, including bits for status indicators like locate, fail, rebuild, and PFA control. It specifies the functional mapping for NPEM command execution and identifies the register’s hardware-initialized capabilities for managing enclosure-specific hardware states.

children.title: Section 7.9.19.4 NPEM Status Register (Offset 0Ch)
children.description: This section defines the **NPEM Status Register (Offset 0Ch)**, detailing the bit-level operations for the **NPEM Command Completed** status, **NPEM Disabled Control**, and **enclosure-specific fields**. It addresses how software manages command execution flow and hardware state transitions within the NPEM controller.

children.title: Section 7.9.20 Alternate Protocol Extended Capability
children.description: This section defines the PCIe Alternate Protocol Extended Capability structure, detailing the headers, status, control, and data registers used to manage non-PCIe protocols over the link. It answers how software discovers, configures, and sequences state transitions for alternate protocol support via specific register offsets and selective enable masks.

children.title: Section 7.9.20.1 Alternate Protocol Extended Capability Header (Offset 00h)
children.description: This section specifies the structural requirements and register layout for the Alternate Protocol Extended Capability, including its mandatory PCIe Extended Capability ID (002Bh) and versioning. It defines the implementation constraints for Downstream Ports and specific Upstream Port functions while detailing the bit-field architecture of the capability header.

children.title: Section 7.9.20.2 Alternate Protocol Capabilities Register (Offset 04h)
children.description: This section defines the **Alternate Protocol Capabilities Register** within the **PCIe Configuration Space**, detailing bit-field layouts for alternate protocol support, **Protocol IDs**, and **VSEC (Vendor-Specific Extended Capability)** mechanisms. It addresses how devices advertise and negotiate non-PCIe protocols, identify supported hardware features, and manage protocol-specific architectural constraints.

children.title: Section 7.9.20.3 Alternate Protocol Control Register (Offset 08h)
children.description: This section details the Alternate Protocol Control and Capabilities registers, defining key technical parameters such as the Next Capability Offset, Alternate Protocol Count, and Selective Enable support. It provides the architectural specifications necessary to determine PCIe capability linkages and the configuration of Alternate Protocol negotiation mechanisms on Link lanes.

children.title: Section 7.9.20.4 Alternate Protocol Data 1 Register (Offset 0Ch)
children.description: This section defines the **Alternate Protocol Data 1 Register (Offset 0Ch)**, detailing the configuration of **Alternate Protocol Negotiation** through bit-fields including **Alternate Protocol Usage Information**, **Alternate Protocol Details**, and **Alternate Protocol Vendor ID**. It specifies **RW/HwInit** attributes for **Downstream** and **Upstream Ports**, providing the technical requirements for link-level protocol discovery and error handling when values return **FFFFh**.

children.title: Section 7.9.20.5 Alternate Protocol Data 2 Register (Offset 10h)
children.description: This section specifies the bit-field definitions, attribute behaviors, and read/write access requirements for the Alternate Protocol Data 2 Register (Offset 10h). It answers technical questions regarding how the register facilitates vendor-defined alternate protocol negotiation, data payload mapping, and protocol-specific status signaling within the PCIe configuration space.

children.title: Section 7.9.20.6 Alternate Protocol Selective Enable Mask Register (Offset 14h)
children.description: This section defines the **Alternate Protocol Selective Enable Mask Register**, detailing how the bit mask controls the negotiation eligibility for specific **Alternate Protocol Index Select** values. It answers how to selectively enable or disable alternate protocols for negotiation and clarifies the register’s dependency on the **Alternate Protocol Negotiation Global Enable** bit.

children.title: Section 7.9.21 Conventional PCI Advanced Features Capability (AF)
children.description: This section details the Conventional PCI Advanced Features (AF) capability structure, covering the function-level reset (FLR) mechanism, transaction pending status bits, and capability header configurations. It addresses how compliant devices manage complex power management and reset transitions through specific registers including AF Control and AF Status.

children.title: Section 7.9.21.1 Advanced Features Capability Header (Offset 00h)
children.description: This section defines the structural layout and register specifications for the Conventional PCI Advanced Features (AF) Capability, focusing on the capability header, AF control, and status registers. It provides the technical requirements for implementing optional root complex integrated functions and explains bit-mask assignments for Alternate Protocol Selective Enablement.

children.title: Section 7.9.21.2 AF Capabilities Register (Offset 03h)
children.description: This section defines the AF (Access Function) Capabilities Register, detailing mandatory bitfields for hardware-level feature support, protocol compliance, and operational constraints. It answers how system software identifies interface-specific functionalities, resource limitations, and supported signaling mechanisms within the configuration space.

children.title: Section 7.9.21.3 Conventional PCI Advanced Features Control Register (Offset 04h)
children.description: This section defines the Conventional PCI Advanced Features (AF) Control Register and AF Capabilities Register, specifying functionality for Function Level Reset (FLR) and Transactions Pending (TP) status. It addresses how software initiates an FLR through the control register and identifies the mandatory hardware capability bits required to support these reset mechanisms.

children.title: Section 7.9.21.4 AF Status Register (Offset 05h)
children.description: This section defines the AF Status Register (Offset 05h), specifically the Transactions Pending (TP) bit, and introduces the SFI (System Firmware Intermediary) Extended Capability for Root Ports and Switch Downstream Ports. It addresses how to interpret completion status and outlines the architectural requirements for firmware-controlled hot-plug mechanisms and ERR_COR Subclass support.

children.title: Section 7.9.22 SFI Extended Capability
children.description: This section details the SFI (System Firmware Intermediary) Extended Capability, a PCIe mechanism enabling firmware to manage hot-plug events and act as a conduit between hardware and the operating system. It defines the register map (including SFI CAM Address/Data registers) and specific requirements for Root Ports and Switch Downstream Ports, such as mandatory ERR_COR Subclass support.

children.title: Section 7.9.22.1 SFI Extended Capability Header (Offset 00h)
children.description: This section defines the structure and bitmask fields of the SFI (Serial Fabric Interface) Extended Capability Header located at offset 00h. It clarifies register mapping, capability IDs, and versioning standards required for identifying and configuring SFI-compliant hardware components within a fabric interconnect architecture.

children.title: Section 7.9.22.2 SFI Capability Register (Offset 04h)
children.description: This section defines the PCI Express SFI Extended Capability structure, detailing the 32-bit Extended Capability Header and the bit-mapped SFI Capability Register. It specifies the configuration space offsets, versioning requirements, and feature flags—such as OOB PD support, Enhanced SFI, and In-Band visibility—necessary for implementing SFI-compliant PCI Express interfaces.

children.title: Section 7.9.22.3 SFI Control Register (Offset 06h)
children.description: This section defines the **SFI Capability Register** (Offset 06h), detailing technical attributes such as **SFI OOB PD (Out-of-Band Presence Detect)**, **Enhanced SFI (eSFI)** support, **SFI CAM** availability, and the **SFI Hidden In-Band** configuration. It specifies register bit functionality for managing hardware-initialized capabilities and identifies how to distinguish between in-band management software access and PCIe-MI operations.

children.title: Section 7.9.22.4 SFI Status Register (Offset 08h)
children.description: This section defines the SFI Status Register, detailing register bit-fields for in-band and out-of-band presence detection (PD), Data Link Layer (DLL) link status, and Device Readiness Status (DRS) notifications. It specifies hardware behavior, read-only/write-1-to-clear (RW1C) attributes, and status reporting mechanisms used to monitor port state transitions and link activity.

children.title: Section 7.9.22.5 SFI CAM Address Register (Offset 0Ch)
children.description: This section defines the hardware specifications for the SFI Content Addressable Memory (CAM) Address Register, detailing its 32-bit register mapping and bit-field assignments. It provides technical guidance on configuring CAM memory access, specifying how to perform exact-match lookups and manage addressing operations within the SFI subsystem.

children.title: Section 7.9.22.6 SFI CAM Data Register (Offset 10h)
children.description: This section outlines the register-level specifications for the SFI (Security Feature Interface) CAM Data and Address registers, detailing access attributes for in-band management versus PCIe-MI interfaces. It defines the operational rules for hardware-based SFI quarantine modes, bit-field configurations, and read/write protocols for managing CAM-indexed address and data registers within the PCIe architecture.

children.title: Section 7.9.23 Subsystem ID and Subsystem Vendor ID Capability
children.description: This section defines the architectural requirements for accessing the Subsystem ID and Subsystem Vendor ID registers via the SFI CAM mechanism. It specifies handling rules for PCIe-MI transactions, Configuration Read/Write requests, and error responses based on the configuration of the SFI CAM Unsupported and SFI RO In-Band bits.

children.title: Section 7.9.23.1 Subsystem ID and Subsystem Vendor ID Capability Header (Offset 00h)
children.description: This section defines the **PCIe Subsystem ID and Subsystem Vendor ID Capability Header**, detailing the **Extended Capability ID**, **Capability Version**, and **Next Capability Pointer** fields at offset 00h. It addresses how the system enumerates and identifies specific add-in card manufacturers and device models to ensure proper driver loading and hardware integration.

children.title: Section 7.9.23.2 Subsystem ID and Subsystem Vendor ID Capability Data (Offset 04h)
children.description: This section details the Subsystem ID and Subsystem Vendor ID (SSID/SSVID) PCI Express Extended Capability structure, defining its 32-bit header and data component registers. It specifies the unique identification fields used to distinguish specific add-in cards and subsystems via the Capability ID 0Dh mechanism.

children.title: Section 7.9.24 Data Object Exchange Extended Capability
children.description: This section defines the Data Object Exchange (DOE) Extended Capability, covering the register-level header structure, subsystem vendor identification (SSVID), and subsystem identification (SSID) fields within PCI Express configurations. It clarifies how hardware implements discoverability and control mechanisms for exchanging data objects across multiple instances of this extended capability.

children.title: Section 7.9.24.1 DOE Extended Capability Header (Offset 00h)
children.description: This section defines the Data Object Exchange (DOE) Extended Capability Header, mapping the structure of the PCI Express Extended Capability ID, Capability Version, and Next Capability Offset. It provides the technical specifications required to identify the DOE function within the PCIe configuration space and determine the link to subsequent capabilities.

children.title: Section 7.9.24.2 DOE Capabilities Register (Offset 04h)
children.description: This section defines the **DOE Capabilities Register**, detailing interrupt notification support via **MSI/MSI-X**, vector assignment logic, and asynchronous messaging capabilities. It answers technical questions regarding how software identifies and configures **DOE event interrupts** and manages the **Message Data offset** or **MSI-X Table entries** for system signaling.

children.title: Section 7.9.24.3 DOE Control Register (Offset 08h)
children.description: This section defines the PCIe Data Object Exchange (DOE) Control Register, outlining bits for aborting transfers, managing interrupts, handling attention mechanisms, and enabling asynchronous message support. It provides technical specifications for controlling DOE instance states, configuration requirements, and operational synchronization via the "DOE Go" bit.

children.title: Section 7.9.24.4 DOE Status Register (Offset 0Ch)
children.description: This section defines the PCIe DOE Status Register (Offset 0Ch), detailing bit-level field definitions for status monitoring, including DOE Busy, Interrupt Status, Error tracking, and Data Object Ready conditions. It specifies operational requirements for error handling, interrupt signaling, and state management during DOE data object exchanges.

children.title: Section 7.9.24.5 DOE Write Data Mailbox Register (Offset 10h)
children.description: This section details the **DOE Write Data Mailbox Register** (offset 10h), specifying register field definitions, data payload formatting, and PCIe **Data Object Exchange (DOE)** protocol requirements. It addresses how initiator-to-target message structures are serialized and provides technical guidelines for managing data flow within the mailbox interface.

children.title: Section 7.9.24.6 DOE Read Data Mailbox Register (Offset 14h)
children.description: This section defines the PCIe DOE (Data Object Exchange) Mailbox registers, detailing the operational mechanics of the **Read Data Mailbox (offset 14h)** and **Write Data Mailbox** interfaces. It specifies the **Data Object Ready** bit behavior, interrupt triggering, and protocol for transferring data objects via firmware/software to manage PCIe configuration or command tasks.

children.title: Section 7.9.25 Shadow Functions Extended Capability
children.description: This section defines the **DOE Read Data Mailbox Register** protocol for structured data object retrieval and introduces the **Shadow Functions Extended Capability** for leveraging **unimplemented Function Transaction ID resources**. It specifies register read/write behaviors and explains how this capability enables a **Requester** to expand its **outstanding Request** limits beyond its primary **Function** allocation.

children.title: Section 7.9.25.1 Shadow Functions Extended Capability Header (Offset 00h)
children.description: This section defines the Shadow Functions Extended Capability Header, detailing the register structure, byte offsets, and bit-level definitions for the Capability ID (002Dh) and Version fields within the PCI Express configuration space. It provides the technical specifications necessary to identify and traverse the Shadow Functions capability hierarchy through the Next Capability Offset field.

children.title: Section 7.9.25.2 Shadow Functions Capability Register (Offset 04h)
children.description: This section defines the **Shadow Functions Capability Register** layout and bit-field definitions at **PCIe configuration offset 04h**. It specifies the control and status mechanisms for managing shadow function mapping, enabling engineers to determine how virtualization-aware hardware exposes or hides underlying device functions.

children.title: Section 7.9.25.3 Shadow Functions Control Register (Offset 08h)
children.description: This section defines the Shadow Functions Capability and Control registers, specifying the implementation count and the enable bit required to authorize a Requester to utilize Shadow Function Transaction ID resources. It addresses how to configure the capacity of the capability structure and the operational state necessary for performing Shadow Function requests.

children.title: Section 7.9.25.4 Shadow Functions Instance Register Entry
children.description: This section defines the Shadow Functions Instance Register Entry, specifying the bit-field mapping and calculation method for the Shadow Function Number using BDF offsets. It also outlines the architectural requirements for the IDE Extended Capability, including its mandatory header, control registers, and the variable-length Link and Selective IDE register blocks.

children.title: Section 7.9.26 IDE Extended Capability
children.description: This section defines the IDE Extended Capability structure, including the Shadow Functions Instance Register Entry, bit-field definitions for BDF offsets, and organizational requirements for Link and Selective IDE register blocks. It provides technical specifications for implementing IDE capabilities in Downstream Ports and Function 0, answering how to configure shadow functions and structure the IDE Extended Capability hierarchy.

children.title: Section 7.9.26.1 IDE Extended Capability Header (Offset 00h)
children.description: This section defines the structural layout and register definitions for the PCI Express Integrity and Data Encryption (IDE) Extended Capability, specifically detailing the capability header format (ID 0030h), versioning, and next-capability pointer. It clarifies how IDE registers are organized, including association blocks for links, streams, and addresses, to enable secure data transmission within a PCI Express fabric.

children.title: Section 7.9.26.2 IDE Capability Register (Offset 04h)
children.description: This section details the **IDE Capability Register (Offset 04h)**, defining bit-level support for **Link IDE**, **Selective IDE**, **Flow-Through IDE**, and **cryptographic algorithm** configurations. It addresses hardware architectural requirements regarding **stream support**, **TC (Traffic Class) limitations**, and the implementation of **IDE_KM protocols** within a PCIe fabric.

children.title: Section 7.9.26.3 IDE Control Register (Offset 08h)
children.description: This section defines the IDE Control Register (Offset 08h), detailing bit-field specifications, interrupt enable functions, and software reset protocols. It answers how to configure low-level IDE interface behavior and manage device-specific control signals using memory-mapped register programming.

children.title: Section 7.9.26.4 Link IDE Register Block
children.description: This section details the Link IDE register block, covering the control and status registers used to manage data stream integrity. It addresses technical specifications for link-level IDE configuration and monitoring, defining how to initiate, track, and troubleshoot stream-specific architectural states.

children.title: Section 7.9.26.4.1 Link IDE Stream Control Register
children.description: This section details the **Link IDE Stream Control Register** configuration within the **PCI Express Base Specification**, specifically addressing control bits for **Link IDE Stream enablement**, **Tx Aggregation Modes**, and **Partial Header Encryption**. It answers how to programmatically manage per-Traffic Class (TC) IDE stream parameters, including algorithm selection and Stream_ID assignment, for flow-through TLP protection.

children.title: Section 7.9.26.4.2 Link IDE Stream Status Register
children.description: This section defines the **Link IDE Stream Status Register**, specifying configuration requirements for **Partial Header Encryption** modes and **AES-GCM 256** algorithm selection. It clarifies synchronization protocols between **Upstream and Downstream Ports** and constraints for modifying settings when the **Link IDE Stream Enable** bit is set.

children.title: Section 7.9.26.5 Selective IDE Stream Register Block
children.description: This section details the configuration and status registers for the Selective IDE Stream Register Block, including Capability, Control, and Status registers. It addresses how to manage IDE stream associations for Requester IDs (RIDs) and memory addresses to facilitate granular PCIe Integrity and Data Encryption (IDE) control.

children.title: Section 7.9.26.5.1 Selective IDE Stream Capability Register
